So... have you come to understand why you came to be robbed?
I'm not stupid.
Though I trust Liang Xun, and therefore I trust you, Master Shen.
*Sigh*... Well, you've got smarts, I'll give you that.
Is that what Liang Xun said about me?
I came to that conclusion myself. Where does Mr. Liang come into this?
He once told me that I put up a front and my predictions never come true, but I have a good eye for people.
That's what I meant.
It's all a matter of experience.
Let's go. This is as high as Huntan Peak gets, and we haven't seen any pavilions on our way up here.
Qujiang Peak isn't far from here, but why pick that one? If we're looking for someone, shouldn't we start where more people live?
What did they used to call Qujiang?
Zuanjiang Peak. It was difficult to write, so they changed it when it got turned into a tourist spot.
Bingo.
I had a dream that I was looking for someone on the peak.
Now if that isn't strange... Sure you didn't learn about the old name on your last trip to Shangshu?
Maybe... Or perhaps...
...I have a mountain spirit guiding me.
You know how to use Arts, right?
Think they call 'em Originium Arts now? An inkhorn of a name, I tell you, but they've always been around, and may as well be the gods and spirits you speak of.
Now that's not quite right, Master Shen. It is man who uses Fulu, and thus it is man who triumphs over nature.
'It is man who triumphs over nature'?
I think I like that saying.
Alright, alright. Originium Arts it is. I don't really get these Originium studies... Hm? What are you spacing out for?
Did I... Did I just say something...?
'Man who triumphs over nature'?
No...
Never mind... I was probably daydreaming.
Qujiang Peak... Zuanjiang Peak.
We looked there numerous times, but the search always came up emptyhanded.
Naturally.
Yet the Sui Regulator has nonetheless come to a decision.
Yes.
Has her presence in Shangshu all these years ever proven to be a transgression, though?
Why, then, did she lend Yan her strength, defending her borders dozens of times?
Over such a long stretch of time, it certainly defies common sense.
But the situation at hand seems to be going against your wishes, Mr. Liang.
If she is a shadow, then the goblet from Lungmen must be a flicker of light.
Did the light come first, or did the shadow? A difficult, yet meaningless question.
What is truly difficult are the people who try to answer this question.
The Sui Regulator came to me directly, to reason with me in person. Naturally, I understand the gravity of the circumstances.
You promised General Zuo that when the cup arrived in Shangshu, you would feign its theft, allowing it to make its way to his son, Lord Zuo.
All to fool certain individuals.
Correct.
Though I couldn't possibly have explained this all to Old Lee, an outsider.
Then you should not have chosen a Lungmenite to handle this.
Given my status, I can't act rashly. It would do more harm than good if it blemishes the early spring festivities.
And as the magistrate, all matters, both large and small, are of the utmost importance to me.
Is he not a friend of yours?
He is my friend, but he is also an outsider.
We can hardly blame you, there are too many eyes.
What of the cup?
As I entrusted my friend with the matter, naturally it is with him.
You wish to have the Lungmenite uncover Ling's whereabouts without lifting a finger yourself?
Correct.
Oh...
Rumor has it that the cup can possess the things around it. Is it true?
Mr. Taihe... I have a question myself.
Please, go ahead.
Let us cast aside our status and speak as equals.
The Sui Regulator bypassed the Ministry of Rites for this matter, far overstepping its bounds. Once the Ministry learns of this, there will likely be quite the quarrel between the two parties.
You are the Deputy Monitor Censor of the Department of Discipline and Supervision. What are your thoughts?
I am indebted to the general, and I am merely repaying the favor.
It comes down to personal feelings, then?
That is correct.
The Department of Discipline and Supervision's Monitor Censor oversees hundreds of officers and champions integrity. It seems to me there should be no place for personal feelings.
If the Department of Discipline and Supervision learns of this and finds the Ministry of Rites in the right and the Sui Regulator in the wrong, what will become of those personal feelings?
Garner loyalty and forsake righteousness.
There wasn't even the slightest hesitation in your answer, Mr. Taihe.
Naturally.
It was the general himself who taught me those words.
Truly a fine man, then.
You needn't worry, however.
General Zuo is a Yanese soldier. He would never do anything illegal.
Did something force the Sui Regulator's hand, then? Even if it means bypassing the Ministry of Rites?
Right... Lord Exorcist Zuo Xuanliao is a man who normally gives the affairs in the borderlands his utmost attention. Who could have diverted his attention to a mere goblet?
It's no trivial matter, but hardly a significant one either.
And I am certain you already know who it is.
*sharp exhale*
Please take care, Mr. Liang.
It seems this spring will be colder than expected.
Here we are.
Aside from a few houses here and there, this is the only town near Quijiang Peak.
What kind of place is this?
Before Shangshu City was built, it was near the border. The mountain was full of mineral resources, and many blacksmiths and stonemasons worked here.
What about now?
With the world at peace, the nomadic cities are all parked around the mountain range. Aside from the few forges kept around as tourist spots, the town is mostly bed-and-breakfasts now.
What lies at the top of Qujiang Peak, then...?
No idea.
Hm... Perhaps just a place for wine and chess?
After all, if it were scenic, there'd be no need to go look for them. Most likely it's a remote place no one ever visits.
I know Shangshu's mounts and waters like the back of my hand... Never heard of any pavilions off the beaten path though.

Qujiang Peak has only an empty swath of land. The legend goes that a god, staring into the deep waters there, attained enlightenment by forgetting the waters surrounding it. From then on, everyone called it the 'Field of Forgotten Water.'
I see...
Sounds like our friend won't be easy to find, then... How about we start with the wine shops? Might be futile without knowing what they look like though.
Any ideas, Master Shen?
When seeking a man, there is this one simple trick that has worked since ancient times.
Have them seek you out instead.
True, but a little passive for my tastes.
I have an idea, though—
Hm...
What is it?
Need something?
Miss Ning...
Reading for leisure, I see. Fine tastes as always, my lord.
Reading for leisure enriches the mind.
If there is something you would like to ask, please go ahead.
My lord, is something weighing on your mind?
Hm... What do you mean?
Your friend from Lungmen.
There's no hiding anything from you, Miss Ning. There is a minor matter, though it's entirely personal.
What does it concern?
We are looking for someone.
Hm... What kind of someone?
An old friend of ours. A practitioner of the martial arts.
I see. Is that all?
His name is Huai.
It seems a simple thing.
I did say it was a minor matter... You needn't worry.
You should hide no secrets from me, my lord.
And I have none to hide.
Very well, then.
The lady before him walks across the room and takes a glance at the bookshelf. She appears engulfed in a sense of defeat.
Fortunately, her mood quickly lightens.
It often changes rapidly.
My lord, the book you were reading... Is it a historical record?
It's about an old Catastrophe that happened within the bounds of Shangshu's Three Mounts.
You seem to be very fond of that piece of history.
I saw it with my own eyes. I can never forget it.
If I remember correctly... 'Tale of the Three Mounts?'
I'm surprised you remember. It seems I've reread that book far too many times.
I only happened to notice.
May I borrow it? I will be paying Uncle Bai a visit. I can read it in my down time.
As you wish.
Very well... But...
Oh, forgive me...
Let me get it for you. I haven't been able to read it much lately, so I placed it fairly high on the shelf.
Thank you.
So you'll be visiting Bai, the potter?
Yes.
Please give him my regards.
Of course.
I will take my leave now.
'Tale of the Three Mounts...'
My lord, I am sure you never realized, but I have actually already taken a peek at your bookshelf, once when you worked late into the night and fell asleep in your study.
(He's never put this book so high up...)
(There was a new case on the shelf too... What could be inside?)

lines.st: I was born in a small village a hundred li away from what is today Shangshu. It was a beautiful oasis.
If you gazed out from the village, you would see a range of mountains that formed a line. The line enveloped a swath of land named Shangshu.
There was a Catastrophe when I was young. The mountains covered a wide area, and the looming clouds covered an even wider area..

The Catastrophe Messengers were quick at their work. As we evacuated, we could see sparks lighting up the sky. All of us knew that Shangshu's Three Mounts would never look the same after the Catastrophe passed.
I was still very young. All I remember is the adults' swaying backs and their forced smiles.
The rescue team moved quickly, but all of us knew that the houses, the farms, and the orchards would all perish in the storm.
We passed a mountain on our way.
With my own eyes, I saw a drunken woman lounging in a pavilion.
I saw her share a toast with the sky.
I saw her fall unconscious in the pavilion, completely inebriated.
But the rescue team didn't stop. The adults around me pretended as though they didn't see her.

lines.x: ...A black goblet.
Was this the cup she drank from...?
Miss Li...
Sir! Do you need something?
Can you describe the guest just now?
Of course... I could never forget a Forte hunk like him...
Prepare two boxes of pastries and pay Miss Ning a visit.
If Miss Ning meets with the Forte there... come back and let me know.
Very well, though why aren't you—Uhh... My apologies, I shouldn't have asked. I will go right now.
Benefactor, I asked around, and it seems we'll arrive at our rendezvous point if we follow this path up the mountain.
But suppose we really don't find anyone there, what should we do?
Then we'll just have to go back to the Liang estate to find Mr. Lee. He said Magistrate Liang asked him to look into the goblet's owner. We should help him out.
But what if Miss Du spoke the truth, and it was indeed a man of renown who seeks the cup...? Would Magistrate Liang truly know nothing of it?
But would that all of this was a mere premeditated conspiracy, why would Magistrate Liang need to go through the trouble and put on such a show?
It's not like we haven't ever seen any big shots ourselves.
You mean the boy who claimed to be a Messenger...?
Haha... If only the Doctor was around. We could've just handed all this trouble off.
*Sigh*... I miss the dorm's soft beds.
*Sigh*... Why must we always mix ourselves up in trouble...?
Finish your tea... It's time to go.
Hm...
Should be around here...
Hot water coming through. Out of the way—
Hm! Watch out!
(Rim Billitonian) —Strewth! Don't scratch the paint, cobba.
A letter? From just now...?
It's past the rendezvous time. They really didn't come.
What should we do now? We don't know if Benefactor Lava has been to the office...
Perhaps we should wait a little longer?
*Sigh*.
Or we could meet up with Mr. Lee first—Hm?
Gkh—!
An Ink Spirit...?!
Hold on, hold on! Can those things leave the scrolls?
I have no idea myself...
Hey, little guy. Where did you come from? Where's your owner?
Gkhaa!
Hey, no biting! Let go! Let go!
You're the one who provoked it.
Bitey, that's enough.
Owie—!
Dusk!
We ran into a bit of trouble. You'll have to bear with me talking to you this way.
Trouble...?
Don't worry about it. It's nothing you can help with.
In Shangshu, we ran into some court envoys who're investigating what happened at Mount Hui-ch'i in Kou-wu City... They said they're from the Sui Regulator.
Tsk... I thought Nian would have everything sorted out by the time she came knocking on my door.
It seems I should've kept my expectations low with the way she goes about things.
Did you fight them?
Heaven forbid we should try to tussle with the mandarins... Not that we would've won if we'd tried, haha...
They mentioned you and Nian, but it didn't seem like they were looking into you two too seriously.
They came to Shangshu on a different mission.
I don't know about that... Do you have any clues?
It's got something to do with a black goblet.
Black... goblet.
We're helping a certain Mr. Lee to look into the cup's master too. The cup looked like it had a pattern on it... Much like the trinket that Nian gave Benefactor Lava.
Heh.
A cup... No wonder something seemed strange... I have a pretty strong hunch as to who that cup belongs to.
She can help us with quite a bit if we actually manage to find her. At the very least, she can help me keep Nian's mouth shut.
Dusk! Get out here already! The old fart's rambling away, he's really pissing me off! It's your turn!
Uhh... Is that Nian?
You must've misheard.
Heavenly forge—
P-Perhaps we should keep you no longer?
You said you're looking for the master of the cup, weren't you?
Find her before the mandarins do. Persuade her to help us.
At the very least, drag this out until we get to Shangshu. Don't let the Sui Regulator take her away.
I'm counting on you.
Hm, we're back...?
Benefactor, is it just me, or does this baffling trickery of Dusk's really come in handy?
It's not worldly Arts anyway, so don't bother trying to learn it.
But it is convenient, I'll give you that.
You're looking for a pavilion?
I'm guessing that a pole-carrier like you, having come up and down the mountains so many times, the range here must be like your own backyard.
Let me tell you, then. The Three Mounts and Seventeen Peaks are full of pavilions, be it old rest stops or newly built scenic spots.
It's tough to find a specific one.
You can do it.
It'll take a lot of work, and I get paid by the trip, you know...
Not like anyone across the world would work for free.
Indeed. Here's your reward, then.
You're from Lungmen?
Yes, what should I call you, kind sir?
The name's Shang... Like Shang-Qie.
What do you want me to do for you? Be your guide? Or do you want me to find somewhere that fits the description and report back?
Time is of the essence. It'll be for the better if we can split up.
You're not from around here... and you're with a boatman?
I've spent a very long time in the rivers, and I am familiar enough with the area.
The rivers are nothing like the mountain roads.
They're both in Shangshu.
You can skip the Bieli, Ziyun, and Qingluan Peaks. They're just small peaks that people back in the old days decided to count toward the Seventeen Peaks.
Very well. We're actually focusing our search today on the Qujiang Peak here.
I'd appreciate if you could find me some clues before sundown... and please do look out for any towns with famous wine shops while you're in the mountains.
Wine?
Aren't all those who frequent pavilions connoisseurs of drink? They're always passed out drunk in them.
I'll ask around, then.
Much thanks.
We'll meet in the teahouse there in the evening, then.
Very well.
See you.
Well, if you aren't a casual guy. You pulled a random person off the street and asked them to help you. Aren't you afraid of getting duped?
I'm a detective. Gotta take a risk every now and then.
Can we trust him?
Just barely. He got attention from all the shopkeeps along the road. That means he's got decent relationships.
A pole-carrier who would swindle this little money wouldn't have such a good reputation in the neighborhood.
Besides, it's better than searching blindly.
You have a point... Where do we head next, then? Any clues?
A mysterious person that our magistrate insists is in Shangshu, their whereabouts unknown nonetheless, wouldn't hang out in a normal town like this.
So much of the problem lies in the cup.
Besides... If someone's bent on getting their hands on this no-name goblet, it only means that this little trinket is the key.
Master Shen, suppose the two of us run into the thieves from the other day. Do you think we can fend them off together?

*Sigh*, I've spent a few decades on the rivers, and the only thing I've ever worried about is how my grandchildren are doing in school. I've never fought anyone, and it would be quite the problem if I ever ran into a bandit.
All I can do is be your tour guide, kid. If you're worried, you'd better go to Mr. Liang soon and have him find you a bodyguard or two.
You haven't ever needed to lift a finger in your line of business?
I've been boating on the rivers for a few dozen years. I've never gotten myself into any of those hairy situations.
I can guarantee your safety if we run into some wild beasts on our journey through the mountains...
But with all the construction around the mountains the past couple years, you won't come across any beasts.
So there aren't any wild beasts... but are there any creatures with metal plates on their backs?
Now what's that about?
I don't know myself.
Where did you see that?
I saw it...
...right here.
This... this is...
Ao!
Hm, it's gone...
You scared it away...
Hey.
Need something...?
Do you have the cup?
We work for Innkeeper Zheng. He has a small eatery in this town as well. He wants to treat you to lunch.
Run!
I really don't understand what Miss Du and the innkeeper are thinking. This is such a pain in the ass...
First, they want us to steal the goods, then they want us to ensure no one makes off with it, and they even want us to get in the employer's way... Hm?
Hey, get up. Take a look over there...
Stop right there...!
Hello, sir. How may I help you?
Uh... Umm... I'm traveling right now, but I lost my wallet. I don't know nobody here. Think you can help me out?
Did all of you lose your wallets together?
N-No, we... uh... we couldn't all be bothered to bring our backpacks with us, you know? So we put our cameras and wallets and whatnot all in one bag. It's the bag that we lost.
We ain't gonna ask much from you. Just head back down the mountain and call the cops at the hotel there. I promise it'll be worth your while. Please, help us.
Look at the time already... Sundown here comes early, huh?
Nothing, mate, how do we get to the Liang estate again?
This way, Benefactor...
Shangshu's streets and alleys are vivid and colorful. There's a certain charm to gazing out at the mountains in the distance and the markets below from atop the skyscrapers here.
It's an entirely different scenery than what you'd find in Kou-wu.
Right. It's got a nice vibe, and the view is great. Not to mention, when the wind blows, you can sometimes smell the rust.
Am I right?
It has been a while since my blade was last unsheathed. How could it possibly rust?
You need us for something?
Of course... Rhodes Island has gotten too involved.
So you've been digging up dirt on us?
Here's a Yanese idiom you might like, Miss Kroos. 'Justice casts a wide net.'
A medical company that strives to rescue all Infected and sees them as equals, regardless of their race, status, and wealth—
—Even the purest of mountain springs must be no match for Rhodes Island in purity.
Thanks for the compliment.
Oh, please don't get the wrong idea... I didn't mean that as a threat.
After all, if Rhodes Island was a dishonest organization, you two would be in jail right now.
In other words, I would like you two to see our current situation as a form of trust.
Should I say thanks?
However, Infected should not be wandering about in such a bustling part of town out in the open. Safety remains a concern, after all.
Would you follow me? Let's take this somewhere else.
(What should we do, Benefactor? This kid's sketchy through and through.)
(Not necessarily, and it's not like we have a choice...)
Sure, let's go somewhere else.
Very well. I see you two are of formidable talent as well. I'll wait for you at the top of the tower to the north in a quarter hour.
Is that what you guys call qinggong in Yanese martial arts?
More or less. Of course, considering his martial prowess, he's no normal man.
Alright, fine... He's extraordinary, actually.
I don't see you get this serious often.
Haha, you jest, my benefactor. I'm always solemn and serious about every last detail.

To possess such fine skills at his age... He must have studied under a distinguished master to hone his extraordinary talent. Even that might be an understatement. His abilities are likely beyond our imagination.
Like I was saying, if he had that bull of a mandarin for a bodyguard, he is not to be taken lightly.
They know about Nian and Dusk. Obviously, they know about Mr. Lee too.
Dusk said... We have to find the goblet's master first.
Getting involved with a Yanese mandarin, though... Amiya's going to chew me out when we get back...
*Sigh*, if only the Doctor... Guess we can't always count on the Doctor, huh...
Well, that was an unfruitful day...
I never had such headaches back at the agency in Lungmen.
Because Mr. Liang left you no leads to follow?
No, it's actually because... I have a lot of friends in Lungmen.
And aside from Mr. Liang the shut-in, the only friend I have in Shangshu is you, Master Shen.
*Sigh*...
There's a crowd at his door, isn't there?
Doesn't look like they're trying to be discreet.
Hey, the Lungmenite's here too. Right... Don't be rash. Just don't let them get away.
Keep an eye on them.
Eh, I don't suppose we walked into their trap?
Weird... It doesn't matter how glorious they were in the old days, they're just a bunch of restaurateurs now. Do they really have this much influence?
There's no one else around—W-Wait!
M-Master Shang.
I'm here.
H-Have a seat. Would you like some tea?
I asked around.
There are a few spots that fit the description. Very few visitors, but there are some pavilions that have been around for a while. I marked them on the map.
As for your wine shops, wherever there's a settlement, it's practically guaranteed that there's one. These places fit the bill.
Hmm...
There's one at Qujiang Peak?
I have no idea myself, but Old Qian insisted that there is.
These are all out-of-the-way trails. No normal sightseers would go there. Be careful.
I see. Thank you, Master Shang.
I will take my leave, then...
Master Shang, do you know those men outside the door...?
Don't know them.
Goodbye.
What's going on...?
(Tell the innkeeper... Master Shang and the Lungmenite have dealings with each other!)
(Should we still block the way out here?)
(What the hell are you even trying to block? Let's go! We're out of here!)
You're back.
That trip was a complete waste. Please don't ask.
I'm just worried about bandits coming after you.
As long as you're looking out for me, they won't make trouble.
What's more, our hot little goblet isn't in my possession anymore. You should be careful yourself.
No need.
We found out who ambushed you. It's all a misunderstanding. They thought you were a smuggler pillaging artifacts.
Well, it sounds like Shangshu's chivalric code is alive and well. That was quite the brawl for a minor 'misunderstanding.'
Don't blame them.
Master Shen, Lee isn't a local. I'm afraid I'll need you to take care of him for the next couple of days.
Of course, it's not big deal.
I know things are hectic at the docks lately. I'll pay you double your wage for your time.
Don't worry about it, Mr. Liang. We never talk money.
I can't do wrong by my citizens.
You've been a great help, and I might need more of your help in the future.
Fine, then... If that's how you feel, then I won't push it.
I'll stick with our boy Lee for as long as he's here. Those of us who make a living on the mountain trails and rivers know the Three Mounts and Seventeen Peaks like the backs of our hands.
Thank you.
—Liang Xun.
What's the matter?
We'll talk inside.
I remember telling you that as long as you aren't hiding anything from me, this will be nothing complicated.
Tell me, what exactly is the story behind the cup and its master?
You can't tell me?
No.
I ran into those guys again in the mountains today.
What...?
So that little 'misunderstanding' of yours isn't actually very convincing.
How high are the stakes?
As high as heaven.
*Sigh*...
You should've told me sooner.
I was afraid you'd get dragged into the mess if you knew too much.
So you still think I'm not part of the mess?
Forgive me.
You should get some rest. If our friend proves too difficult to locate... then you don't have to worry about it.
We've come this far. You can't suddenly have second thoughts about this, can you?
I appreciate the feeling, but if I really call it quits here, that's the end of the good life for you. I know that much.
So what's on the table? You can at least tell me that, can't you?
Both the people and the country.
Well, the stakes do sound high, and it's such a tall tale, I'm finding that kind of hard to believe.
I would never joke about such a serious matter.
Of course I know you wouldn't.
Let me change the subject, then. You wouldn't be entertaining a lovely lady this evening, would you?
—*Cough*.
There is nothing between me and Miss Ning.
I never said there's anything between you two.
Your eyes said enough.
Really? Everyone always ridicules you for how unsociable you are, but even Huai Tianpei's girl is all grown up now. You have to have scored a home run at least once or twice?
You have your reasons, huh?
How about a drink?
It's high time we had a drink together.
Right... We're long overdue.
You truly are a swift one, Miss Kroos. The innkeeper wasn't kidding.
Where is the man with the fan?
We couldn't risk a complete wipeout in case you had any traps set up here, can we?
How peculiar... I thought you would send him, while you hide in the shadows.
As the saying goes, one should sacrifice a pawn to save one's queen. Not only are you the 'queen,' you're a crossbowman and an escape artist.
Even so, you came personally.
Well, my mate Nothing isn't a regular operator yet. I can't just have him fly into the face of danger.
And yet you still have a mind for danger, especially when you have to go it alone, don't you?
If you're the kind of bloke who goes all 'I can see right through you' just to seem smart, I'd rather go home and have a sleep.
Haha... so temperamental. Forget I said anything, then.
I'll get to the point. We've looked into Rhodes Island a little. With so little time, we didn't dig up a lot of information.
I don't claim to fully know you, but at least know you can be trusted.
Can I? Not only did I butt my head into this affair, I'm an Infected, you know?
You must put the right people to the right job. Oripathy is a misfortune that all Infected face. What does that have to do with their responsibilities or skills?
I would like Rhodes Island to do us a favor.
No, in fact, if you don't help us, then I'm afraid you'd be making an enemy of us.
You want Mr. Lee's goblet.
When I chased the thief that night, right away, the tracks split into two. It was dark out, and you moved quick, but I never took my eyes off the one who'd actually left the Liang estate.
I lost them not long thereafter, though. And then a certain someone showed up, just like it was a planned diversion—
The innkeeper drew my attention. Was he drawing it away from you?
No wonder he thinks so highly of you.
Bingo, eh?
What does the cup have to do with ‘Sui?'
You know of 'Sui?'
Oh... Looks like you're not lying, though you know only half the story.
Hm, is that so?
Considering you see Nian and Dusk as friends... and that she told you about this much, they must see Rhodes Island as a lifeline of sorts.
In years past, so long as they stayed in line and broke no rules, the Sui Regulator would turn a blind eye to whatever they did.
But nowadays, things are different.
Not only are the times changing, you are on friendly terms with Nian, and you even brought Dusk off her mountain. That put you on the Sui Regulator's wrong side.
We were just...
I know you meant no ill will, but what comes next?
How is Great Yan meant to know that they are not planning anything when they are all in one place?
'Great Yan...'
I'm not trying to exaggerate.

Nian and Dusk disappeared not long after they reached Shangshu, and it just so happens that a goblet associated with them made its way here around the same time. Furthermore, the man of the hour is on Rhodes Island's side.
This is a tremendous coincidence. Wouldn't you say so, Miss Kroos?
And I'm not one to believe in coincidences.
Oof!
I forgot to hang the clothes to dry. Oh no, oh no... If I don't get to that now, there won't be anything to wear tomorrow.
Shang Zhong, huh? You shouldn't have come.
Now get lost. I'll pretend I didn't see you.
There was a young man who asked me to look for a pavilion in the mountains.
We all know things were complicated when you left, but not only did you leave us without an explanation, you even set the innkeeper up. Going by the rules, that makes you a traitor.
Then your men surrounded him.
Please, traitor, Brother Shang, I'm begging you. If anyone sees us, things will get hairy. Let's put a stop to this.
Who is that man?
Brother Shang!
I'm asking you, who is that man?
The Lungmenite...?
Yes.
It's all business. It's got nothing to do with outsiders, and I have nothing to tell you.
It'll be ten full years at the end of this one. I've been thinking of giving the Association an explanation soon.
I'll give all of you an explanation.
I'll settle the score with Zheng Qingyue.
I still can't tell you, Brother Shang!
Liu! Who pulled you out of the fire all those years ago?!
I can't say.
It has to do with the goblet, doesn't it? The cup from ten years ago?
You—
Bingo, then...
So that's the 'goblet' the kids have been talking about...
Hey, you didn't interrogate any of the young disciples, did you—
This must be fate...
—Hey, Shang Zhong! Don't just leave after you got that answer from me! Our years of friendship mean nothing to you, huh?! Hey!
Hah, looks like you're a better drinker than you used to be.
The Liang Xun of the good old days would probably be curling up into a ball under the table right about now.
I drink by myself every now and then.
Well, that's our Magistrate Liang. You find the time to drink even as a government official—Hold on, you drink by yourself every now and then. That doesn't mean what I think it means, does it?
I drown my sorrows. It's human nature.
They say that it takes a personality change to build your tolerance for alcohol. Wise words, don't you think?
It's a fair point.
*Sigh*... When we drown our sorrows, it's a whole myriad of relationship we're trying to drown.
To tell you the truth, I was actually pretty surprised when I saw you again.
Hm? Why is that?
The Liang Xun I know is a methodical, taciturn fellow.

I remember that time in school, outside the Chamber of Teachers Emeriti, when you said you were going to bless all under heaven. You said the world's a harsh, cold place, so you'd build a fire to warm everyone up, and they would never know darkness again.
I was just spouting words. We were young. Perhaps those were the exact words that led me to reflect on myself now.
It was years ago.
Many years ago.
You aren't the refined young master you used to be either.
Time flies, and I frittered all mine away.
It's been a long time since you left home. Have you spoken to your family?
It hasn't been that long since I left home.
You're saying that detective agency in Lungmen is your home now?
Of course.
What about back in Jiangdong?
Jiangdong, or Kong Tung as we call it, is just a place with a house, some lanterns, and a few old friends.
We've both changed.
I hope that when we clink our glasses together, it's not the sound of our past ambitions crumbling away that we hear.
No, no, no, that's not quite right. You're a mandarin of some standing and the magistrate responsible for a city. If the masses are happy with you, perhaps you've already achieved your dreams?
We still have a long road ahead of us. Shangshu has always been a bustling place. What I'm trying to accomplish is just gilding the lily.
I hope that you won't forget who we once were, then. Gon bui.
Gan bei.
Seems like Yan's research into occult mysticism is the greatest secret across this whole world.
You make it sound like we don't want to share.
They may take different forms, but all nations approach the same problem in their own way. Everyone faces this issue.
Though, because of certain... historical reasons, Great Yan's Sui Regulator happens to be at the forefront of 'Feranmutology.' Always has been. Always will be.
Kjerag still worships them as gods and Sami attempts to coexist with them, but the Sui Regulator's research has advanced to unparalleled heights.
So, the 'Sui' in Sui Regulator is the same 'Sui' that Nian talked about?
Did Nian tell you about those legends? The old emperor, the wild hunt, and what they uncovered?
(That's the stuff Lava talked about...)
In ancient times, the people thought of certain creatures as 'gods,' and with civilization's advancement, they gradually began to go into hiding.
The 'Sui' is one such creature.
'Certain creatures...?'
Whether they actually meet the definition of a living being is up for debate, and I don't suppose we need to go there.
All you need to know is that when at last they come to bring destruction to the world, Yan has the power to annihilate them. That's all.
Fair enough...
This is not exactly confidential Sui Regulator information, but it's not exactly common knowledge either.
I'm telling you all this not because I want a scholarly discussion.
Nian, Dusk, and the one who's in hiding somewhere in Shangshu. All of them are under the Sui Regulator's surveillance.

Normally, these things would not happen at such a pace. But then one day, the clouds all dissipated from that mountain. By the time the Sui Regulator Candleholders arrived, Dusk was nowhere to be found.
That happened just a few days ago.
It took only a few days for the Candleholders to brief the court on this matter too.
That's something... There's quite a bit of distance between Mount Hui-ch'i and the capital, isn't there?
Rhodes Island is getting itself involved without knowing the whole picture. The ignorant are blameless. On the contrary, it's unimaginable that you would work with those two without knowing anything.
And now, we would like Rhodes Island to follow the Sui Regulator's arrangement and persuade the Lungmenite to surrender the goblet himself.
I knew it... Liang Xun was under your thumb the whole time.
Correct.
He had Mr. Lee deliver the cup all the way from Lungmen to be stolen, and it's all a show, huh?
You call the magistrate by name, but gave the Lungmenite a courtesy title. That shows you are a good person.
Does being a good person mean anything else in Yan?

Please don't get the wrong idea. I'm not trying to beat around the bush. All I'm saying is that you aren't hiding your trust in the Lungmenite anymore. Is it because of his partnership with Rhodes Island?
It's actually more than that... He's a friend too.
There aren't many people we can truly call friends. It seems Nian and Dusk are your friends too.
It sounds like the Sui Regulator has some high standing in Yan. So why the ruse? Who are you trying to fool?
I'm afraid that's more than what Rhodes Island needs to know.
And you straight up admit that?
There's nothing to hide. It's just a more elegant way to go about this to save our mandarins some face.
Now that I've told Rhodes Island so much, can we set aside our differences and negotiate?
You want our help?
We'd like Mr. Lee's too.
What do you need help with?
The elimination of the Sui.
I'm not drunk. No need to hold me like this.
You're my guest.
I'm just a grunt you summoned from Lungmen.
I actually considered the possibility that the Messenger might not be able to find you in Lungmen.
And I actually registered my agency in Lungmen hoping that you would find me.
'Lee's Detective Agency.' Why did you choose to build your business in Lungmen, though?
It's the farthest away I could get from Yan.
Any farther than that... I have no place to go.
You could come back.
I don't want to.
Liang Xun.
Hm...
I know, actually.
You always knew.
Lungmen is not your home, yet you spent half your life holding out hope for Lungmen.
I thought, there, I could finally find what it was that I wanted to do.
Did you?
I did, but I also found that I could find it just about anywhere.
All those years ago, Huai Tianpei said he'd study kung fu and make a name for himself.
And all those years ago, Liang Xun wanted to do something for all people under heaven, to change the ways of the world such that they would no longer have to be afraid.
What about you?
I was just a finbeast in a river, and the moment I jumped a few chi out of the water, I felt I was at the top of the world.
Okay, I'm pooped. I've got a long day ahead of me tomorrow. You should get going.
Very well.
Take care.
There's no more wine...
*Sigh*.
Old friends, old friends...
Hmm...
...A brilliant move.
I see that I'm losing. I suppose there is no need to continue?
That's right.
There is no need to continue.
There are more to the four arts than weiqi, and yet that's the one you always pick to challenge me. This is a form of bullying.
One man must avoid one's shortcomings.
And we both know I am no match for you in poetry and song.
Fair.
Lost interest already?
These are times of peace.
Though these times of peace are enjoyed only by Yan.
Your stones are set only in Yan.
Correct. The board is entirely within Yan's borders.
Though I am not necessarily playing every round. In that case, the other games must take place elsewhere in the world too.
Will those games have aught to do with you?
Real men remain silent as they spectate and never regret their moves.
So all you do is spectate. You watch mankind as they wage wars and battle with each other.
I'm just learning the art of the game by spectating. Even children start to play by imitating.
Hm...
But you are not him.
What do you mean?
You... Oh, you are one of his pieces.
Will you play in his stead, then? Mr. Lee of Lungmen?
Hoh... This is peculiar.
Why did he drag an ordinary man like you into this game?
No, perhaps he didn't choose you in particular. He simply realized that things would come to this sooner or later, no matter who and no matter what?
What are you talking about?
I'm saying you are not the disheartened man, but you are sitting here in his stead, playing a game with me.
Who are you...?
Ling.
What is this place?
A dream... from a hundred years ago.
Now that's funny. How could I have a dream that takes place a whole century ago...?
Indeed...
How could you?

lines.st: What are you doing here?!

lines.x: Hmm... It feels like I had quite the dream... Oh?
Stop right there!
That voice...

lines.st: It's nearly dawn.
I suddenly realize, ever since I came to Shangshu, I haven't had a proper sleep.
There's a great commotion outside. It seems two people are fighting.
But I know their fight isn't because of each other. They're fighting over me, and that cup.
I've always considered myself fine at reading people. I've guessed, too, that this can't all be that simple.
My premonitions have never been far off the mark either. So I've prepared two letters, sat on the table.

lines.x: Let's not trouble our Mr. Liang for now.
But I am curious. Are you just allowed in or out of the Liang estate whenever? Liang Xun might not put on government airs, but this has to be a little too careless.
*Sigh*...
Stop there!
(Rim Billitonian) Crikey, my list didn't have nothin' about this sheila!
Hmph. Nowhere to run.
Who sent you?!
Wait, you're not even Yanese! Where did you come from?
What's going on?
Here I was thinking you kicked the bucket in your sleep.
You're...
(Are these beasts long-snouted slumberfoots?)
She was clearly scheming something outside your room. You think do-gooders do that?
(Rim Billitonian) I'm not secret here anymore, so it's in for a penny, in for a pound...
Er, okay, Rim Billitonian woman, hold your beasts...
This is the Shangshu magistrate's land. I don't mind either way, but getting on the court man's bad side won't do you any favors.
(Rim Billitonian) You're able to rabbit on in the ol' Rim Bil?
Roughly.
May I have your name, young woman? And what are you paying your very bold visit here for?
Ye Ban. Blacknight.
Why were you ignoring me if you could speak Yanese?!
You're a Rim Billitonian, but you have a Yanese name?
That's not the point. I really don't end up in these situations, but I'm not looking for too much trouble in this city anyway.
A hunt with no traps and no plan is a hunt just waiting to fail.
Give it up. You're not pulling any schemes here with me on the scene.
Miss Du's a temperamental sort.
So you two are working together.
This works out just fine, actually. While you've been focusing on me...
Light, Drill!
Wrroooaw—!
(There's more slumberfoots!)
Gah! It's got the goblet in its mouth!
See you never!
Hold it right there!!
Hey! What dimension are you living in? After her!
Long-snouted slumberfoots don't live in Yan, let alone keeping a cadre of tame ones as your partners in crime. This woman's probably come chasing us from somewhere else entirely.
Hmm...
What are you still thinking about? If I didn't help you, she would've trapped you to death right there!
No, I wouldn't say she was after my life.
Cool, you lost the goblet, and you don't even act like you care. Aren't you scared Mr. Liang's gonna chew you out?
Now I've lost the cup, and your hopes have come true. Isn't that just dandy?
Tch... Fine, I said that, but if this spirals out of our control, what do we do then?
I just wanted my dad to suck it for once. I didn't think the whole Association would be done for.
Where's Liang Xun?
Benefactor, this... matter you raise... it's far beyond the pale. You'll have to let me absorb this slowly.
Don't blame you. I've known Nian forever, but I still need some time to take this in.
And you told me without the slightest reservation.
You're a Rhodes Island operator too.
Right you are, benefactor.
But... whether or not we agree with it, having a nation's top organizations watching Rhodes Island so closely... doesn't make for easy sleeping at night.
The Sui Regulator, the Ministry of Rites. Just their names alone are intimidating.
Yet I didn't quite pick up on any malice when they spoke to us, you know?
Perhaps we've taken 'Nian's household affairs' too simply.
Eugh. Should at least have Amiya and the Doctor know about this before we make any calls...
But it's not like Closure and Amiya'd have no idea about this, right...? But they agreed to Nian's requests too, back then...
And who knows if Lava's given the branch office a shout yet.
Benefactor, given things are currently brewing up a storm, perhaps we should put our own safeties first, don't you think, or...?
We'll discuss this with Mr. Lee first. Nian be damned, but Dusk's not somebody who makes things mysterious on purpose.
If what she said was true, then it looks like this whole thing has a lot more moving parts than we thought...
A safe proposition. That's my benefactor.
You say that when Yan's Sui Regulator's already come knocking. Don't think safe's the day of the word here—
Benefactor...? What has you furrowing your brow?
This thing's—
Ao!
—! Nothing, move it!
...Oho!
Wh—What in the world...?!
Fphhh...
What? Is this some... metal animal?
W—Wait, that's a teapot, that's a painting frame... Are these things alive—?!
Where did these things crawl out from—h-hold on, has that teapot grown legs?
Wrroooaw—!
Wargh!
Watch out, benefactor!
A fair, square hit!
(Wails)—!
Looks like that works—!
—Slow down, this—thing's turned back into a common paperweight...?
(Damn—they're all over the place here—!)
Aaaaow!
Urgh!
—Benefactor!
What's happening? You're making lots of noise here... um, uh, why's that flowerpot moving...?
Waaaagh—what's going on?! What are these things?!
Where did this miscellany all come from...?!
(Oh! What did I just step on?)
Did this—fall out of my benefactor's bag?
Pfhh?
Nothing!
Ao—!!
Geh!
What are you all charging at me for?!
There's one more!
That one was narrow! Much thanks, benefactor!
Ow—ow, ow...
But what could possibly be happening? Could there possibly even be more scoundrels working against us?
I've got this feeling I've seen these things somewhere before...
Erk, you're right! They practically seem an homage to Miss Dusk's Ink Spirits!
Fphhh...
Wargh! Were there still leftovers?
They're all coming at the charm Nian gave to Lava. That settles it—
—They have to be pretty much like Ink Spirits!
Liang Xun...!
Lee.
The cup got stolen?
I was careless. It seemed to be a beastmaster's tame beast that did it...
Wasteland long-snouted slumberfoots have a special organ in them. There's some kind of hypnotic substance they can secrete.
But none of the sort live in Shangshu. Someone skilled, strong, and in command of beasts that put people to sleep? A fine accomplice if you're out to steal a cup.
And the thief's not even with the inn people.
You ascertain that much?
You're not surprised at all. You figured it out long before I could, I'm guessing?
The look on your face tells me this one was definitely unexpected.
Any immediate suspicions?
I didn't tell anyone at all where the cup was hidden.
I think you told someone else I had it on me.
I did indeed say that.
That's good, then.
But look at this study. Completely neat and tidy, just missing that case you hid the cup in.
So who can get into your study, and who could send someone to sticky-finger the cup on such short notice?
—Don't—Don't tell me too much. I'm scared I'll get my feet caught in your bureaucratic vines.
Do you need my help to bring the one behind that thief to light?
Reclaiming the goblet should come first.
Moreover, hunting that one down... would be too hasty.
Hey, little girl.
I have a name!
You're so slow about everything... What did Mr. Liang say to you?
Some quite perplexing things, but absolutely not to do with your old dad this time.
Tch...
Seems our Miss Du Yaoye does like her father after all.
You said how you were all for young against old, and how you didn't even want to take this job, but you've got other ideas too, don't you?
N-No talking about me for now! You know what's happening, so think how we can haul her back in, quick!
Easy enough.
That Blacknight had real freak skills. You think it's so simple to get her?
We're just in luck. The better the skills, the easier they take the bait.
But what's important is, as a Rim Billitonian, how she chased her way into Shangshu. And who it is controlling her behind the scenes.
She's not from Lungmen?
I didn't hurry too fast from Lungmen to Shangshu. If she really was out tracking me down, she could've pulled things off at any Messenger station. Why wait to move until I'm on the Liang estate?
Not to mention, a Rim Billitonian wantonly raiding a Yan magistrate? Either she's an idiot, or someone's using her.
Hah. Doesn't that make her an idiot either way?
Not necessarily. People do act naive at times.
When?
When they want something from someone very badly.
Are you trying to tell me something?
You're imagining things.
Will Rhodes Island really do something just because you left that letter in your room?
We have a level of trust.
Do you know them that well?
Good people always know each other well.
His door's open... but where's Mr. Lee?
Benefactor, there's two letters here. It seems one's meant for us.
Let's see...
Benefactor, I admit I've been holding in my curiosity. Just what kind of person is this Brother Lee?
...Err.
He's a detective. Had dealings with Rhodes Island since a while ago now... well, mainly the kids from his agency have.
But little by little, him and us have gotten familiar.
I see, a detective... no wonder he's so meticulously minded. By his wardrobe, I would've guessed him to be some manner of tianshi with a mild hand for Originium Arts.
What did Brother Lee write, then?
Says...
Read it for yourself.
'Cup was taken. Find Master Shen at ferry crossing,' and...
W... Well, he really hasn't taken us for outsiders. Master Shen would be that boatman of some admittedly suspicious intrigue, no?
If we don't get this sorted properly, Rhodes Island can kiss goodbye to not getting involved. Better we listen to Mr. Lee for now.
He predicted this situation'd get more complex, so that's why he didn't want us involved at the beginning... Does this really mean he saw all this coming?
How come you're out of things to say now? Really? We haven't run that far yet.
And can you tell me how you know they're gonna listen to your letter at all?
If I start telling a youngster like you, Miss Du, it'll be your fault when you get bored.
You love pretending you're so mature... That's why I hate all you kung fu nutjobs.
You've got the wrong idea there. I run a licensed detective agency.
Does being the boss of one guarantee you're a detective?
Er...
What, you're wasting all this effort just to find this goblet's owner and nothing else? You swear?
Of course there's more. I want my own answers, too.
Y-You know, if what you say is true, couldn't that Blacknight bandit seriously just leave Shangshu?
A thief with knowledge of how Magistrate Liang's study is laid out? She must be somewhere nearby, off to meet her puppet master.
And on the off chance she scrams?
If a Rim Billitonian can get out safely after stealing the magistrate's property, then someone wants her leaving—or to put it the other way around, she's going absolutely nowhere.
Guaranteed?
Not that guaranteed, actually. It'll just be a gamble.
Then we're too late then, aren't we?!
If worst comes to worst and she did scarper on us, then with your help, we'll just get her back.
Easy for you to say! How am I meant to know which way she went?
Well, if they've come prepared, then how do you find a single person in a Shangshu-sized haystack? Is one pair of eyes and one pair of legs going to do the trick for you, Miss Du?
Then what can... wait...
It's not me who wants that lousy goblet, either.
Someone's stolen it? Brilliant. We'll go see just what fins those anglers are trying to fish up with it.
Do you already have guesses for who they are...?
All I'll do is ask is that you all take your seats.
Very rare you should call for me so early in the morning, my Lord.
Miss Ning...
The camellias in the roadside garden have bloomed. Have you seen them?
You were the one who planted them.
Yes, much thanks to your tender care for them.
You truly let your own garden run wild. 'Tis become a means for me to kill my time.
I will sprinkle a few red plums next time, then.
As you wish.
Why such agitation, my Lord?
There's something I wish to consult you on, Miss Ning.
Well, will you say it? Since when could you no longer say things straight, Liang Xun?
It's to do with that friend of mine.
Do you not trust me?
I have always trusted you, Miss Ning.
Really? If so, why do you ask to confirm before you speak?
Er. You are completely right...
Though I do not mind the walk to see you, 'twould not be just to ask me some questions that you called for me, so rare that is?
If I have displeased you, I humbly apologize.
Haha. Why the nervousness? Am I so frightening?
Yesterday morning, I went to old Zhao's jewelers', and had repairs on the bracelet you gave me. At noon, I went to Miss Wen's shop to pick furniture; I have long wanted a larger dining table...
After, I went with Miss Wen to meet her husband at the dock.
In the afternoon, we ate our meal at Chef Hu's, talked of everyday domestics...
Finally, I went to Uncle Bai's, watched him teach his potter apprentices, and went on a stroll with him.
Is there anything you still wanted to ask, my Lord?
The bracelet I gave you was just an ordinary silver one. It's not of great worth.
Really, may I not like it if it is not of great worth?
I hear Uncle Bai has had a cold the last few days. He's admittedly getting on in years; he's at the point he should be minding his health.
He will.
But Uncle Bai has been pottering in Shangshu for ten years now, every afternoon and for six hours. Thunder could not move him.
Habits become nature; there will be no exhorting him now.
You met all five of them, throughout all of yesterday?
'Tis routine matters.
I understand now.
So, now you have finished questioning me, you shall next be heading out, my Lord, shall you not?
It's almost daybreak.
'Tis early to be visiting the theater, no?
Not a suitable time, indeed.
And you know my body is frail...
I will go and brew some tea, then. You wait.
Mm.
Oh, yes. Miss Ning, the book you borrowed yesterday from my study... have you finished reading it?
It's day now.
But the snow still hasn't melted.
I have something I need to ask you too.
What?
Do you see these... little whatsits multiplying?
Phhh... ao!
What is this?
Fphhh...
If these things appear because of that goblet...
Then that means we bet on the right street.
(...They haven't followed me? I'm finding this too easy.)
(Let's see this letter... that storyteller has seemingly changed profession? Pottery? Pottery what...)
Where are you going?
—Who is that?!
Why is that 'unaccounted-for' goblet in your grasp?
Gkh...
Ao?
Wrrao!
Gkh... gkh...?
Gkh...
Ao...
So you *are* here.
The breeze in the mountains is ever crisp and cool.
And mornings as refreshing as these are always fine for settling muddy, murky matters.
In a mountain-sat village, when kitchen's smoke has just sprung up, the snow still unreceded in spring, one old and one young stand off against each other.
The only sound, the single strike of a faraway gong.
You've kept me waiting.
Tch. Bloody lout... Light, stay cool for the moment.
I've seen you before. Hmm. So it was you.
Unexpected, for sure. A Rim Billitonian, silently lurking in Shangshu since the very beginning.
I can't understand you, sorry.
Hmph.
Light!
What—?!
A snare?
You train these slumberfoots well. An ordinary woman would be headfirst in the ground by now.
(He didn't just notice the snare behind him, he stepped on the rope without hesitation... Bastard...)
Don't worry, I won't make this hard for you, girl.
Do you know what that goblet you're holding is?
It's not my concern, or my interest. It's newbie bounty hunters that ask too many questions.
Then you have only yourself to blame.
Don't get so full of yourself! Helmet! Drill!
If you don't know what it is, then be wise and hand it over. Don't be used for nothing.
H-How dare you do that to Helmet—! You asked for it!
...A headless bolt.
(H-He caught it barehanded? At this distance?! And I *just* changed the string on my crossbow—!)
Fine.
Seeing how you're unwilling to take my life, I'll give you one chance.
Hand over the goblet to me, and then tell the one instructing you: Tread carefully. Don't be selfish.
Hmph. You know who hired me?
Whether I know or not doesn't matter. What matters is I can stop you, right here.
Why should I listen to you?
You have no choice.
Maybe you can run, but you could never bear to see these beasts of yours meet a sorry fate.
You're threatening me?
You have skill. But aside from your crossbow, you've got nothing in a fight.
You can pull out before you're in too deep. You need a good eye and the right people, if you want to survive here.
True. They never told me someone as capable as you would get in the way.
If I had someone do something dangerous for me, I wouldn't give them the whole picture either.
It's just a job to you, stranger. Why stake your life on it?
Give the cup to me, and I vow on the Escorts' name that whatever they're paying you, we'll double it.
Hah, I was taking a loss anyway with all my volunteer work.
Call this a favor for a friend.
A friend.
So even you bandits of the wild don't mind a sacrifice for a friend.
Sounds like you've got something against us.
No... not at all.
The longer you do business, the more fine people you meet, the less friends you end up with.
Then be smart, and go back to your business.
Yes, business is always easiest done in the mountains.
Three Mounts, Seventeen Peaks, hundreds of passes. Is there any road without a teahouse and station for travelers to rest their feet? Is there any road... without our people?
I hope you see clear now, girl.
You really don't think anything of me.
A direct confrontation might not be my style, but you've pissed me off just far enough... isn't that right, my darlings?
(Rim Billitonian) The old todger wants to have a go, let's give 'im a go! We've been around the paddock a few times, we're no strangers!
*Sigh*...
Some just refuse to learn.
If that's your answer, then you'll pay the—
Even if a third party was stood there watching, they wouldn't have a clue just how the coming step came to land.
Simply put, the man is adept in walking passes, in climbing mountains. The steps he lifts and places, day after day after day, are truly too many to count.
In one clean noise, clay bricks shatter in the ground. Before him, dust drifts down. By his gaze is a simple, common bamboo-wood carrying pole. His gaze itself, faint rage.
Far away, the gong strikes again.
—H-How... Where did he come from... Wait, Light, calm down! And Drill! Back to me...!
...It's you.
You finally came to the mountains.
So I came to find you.
*Sigh*...
What comes will come. Nothing's avoidable forever. Is there a need to be so hasty?
Hey. Miss.
...Are you talking to me?
(His movement—is that wooden stick one of their carrying poles? What did he do just now... to shatter bricks made of stone?)
Do you know him?
No.
Then there's some problem between you.
I only see you between us.
You're right. I'm your problem, then.
When I'm done here, I'll come see you. But not now.
Miss. Does this relate to the item in your hands?
Uh... it does...
This is personal business. And major at that. Stay out of it.
How major?
The safety of the nation is at stake! Life and death!
Since that rainy night those years ago, I've rarely heard you use such a tone.
I see. How could you be the villain here? What a chivalrous swordsman. What a dutiful innkeeper.
Then get out of the way—
—But I know just who you are, and that's why you'll never see me.
And if you won't, then it changes nothing if I find you. That's why I never came down from the mountains.
So. If...
Hey! You—!
Give that back!
It was ten years.
Ten years ago, you watched as my son died... so you could protect this goblet.
Shang Zhong!
Miss. This is nothing to do with you now.
You steal my things while I'm not looking, and then tell me it's not my business?!
This was never yours.
Hmm...
Shang Zhong, have you lost it?!
You're free to go now.
Shang Zhong!
Leave!
Grr...!
Mark my words, you two... I'll be back for it!
Zheng Qingyue... Where's your blade?
At home.
I see. At home.
That rainy night, in the escort, over a dozen lost their lives. There was a boy among them, and a father.
The boy was a Shang. My boy. The father was a Du. Du Yaoye's father.
That's why you don't carry your blade.
What a tremendous joke.
You still hate me to this day.
I don't hate you. I know you didn't mean to. The blame lies with you, but no fault.
'Save the goods, then the men,' hm?
Yet the goods are gone now, and so are the men. Am I still not allowed to condemn you?
I must ask you give justification, or else I cannot live on.
I will. Give me the goblet. Do you know what it entails—
No. He died for this cup then. I'll bury it beside him today.
I'll be waiting atop the mountain, for you to fetch your blade.
Master Shen!
Mm? What brought you over here? I was about to look for you at Liang's.
Where's young Lee?
The goblet got stolen. Mr. Lee sent us to fetch you.
Did he tell you anything about this?
He asked you to come get me, but not to first find Mr. Liang?
To be clear, you came from the Liang residence?
Er... yeah.
...*Sigh*. The fact he didn't send you to find Liang Xun means deep down, he already knows...
Gifted, how gifted. How truly gifted.
You're back, Uncle?
How were things?
In regards to her, thoughtful of etiquette, true as can be.
If she was so ready to display etiquette, that shows there's still room for mediation.
This is regardless of how she acts, or what intentions she harbors. Master Tai's instructions lie here, and they can't be false.
She's clever.
Since I visited in an official's capacity, she should have realized to some extent.
Very well.
Both sides are now strained to breaking point. We stand on a hair trigger.
We can only resolve this incident before we draw even more dispute.
If we really do want everyone to trust that our methods can forever rid Yan of the sword hanging over her head, then we naturally must obey those magic words—
—Frank and forthright.
One noon tea.
Alright! Take a seat, Master Shang!
Why so late to work today?
Met an old acquaintance in the morning.
Which acquaintance?
Lungmenite.
Fine day. Fancy meeting you again, Master.
What, still looking for that pavilion?
You helped me considerably by narrowing things down, but I'm still meant to find a person and not just a pavilion, so there's yet more to do.
Is that your sweetheart, sitting over there?
What...?!
Ahem. No, I'm not that brave...
Oh, your daughter, then?
I hardly seem the fathering kind, don't I?
That's true.
Hey, what did you just say?! Me and that Lungmenite conman are what?!
A man and woman going up the mountains at this hour, in early spring. Nothing else to do, probably.
I—
Master Shang, your noon tea.
Thank you.
Noon tea? Not sure if it's noon yet.
Every Shangshu pole-carrier gets two free servings of tea in the mountain teahouses. A noon tea in the morning, a sunset tea in the afternoon, each with some small sides.
The taste of a free tea is always fine.
It's tea, nothing more.
But it's in the mountains, see. Mountain tea's always a bit more expensive. And if it's complimentary, you've made out like a bandit.
You manage your books well.
Are the shopkeepers really that kind, though? Is it all out of benevolence?
The authorities subsidize it, duh.
...And you'd know?
My home happens to be a food service establishment too. Of course I've got some idea.
...I see.
Are you close with the Shangshu authorities?
Have been since Mr. Liang took up office.
The last few Shangshu governors were about construction, civil projects, developing the mountains and forests—not just recovering the city from all its troubles, but breathing even more life into it.
Maybe Governor Liang Xun thought the city was renewed enough when he came in, because his agenda was peace and love for the citizens instead... Whatever you think of this, you've seen nothing yet.
You're wise, for your young age.
My dad does business. It's all just osmosis.
You have a fine father.
I've finished my tea. I should be on my way now.
See you again, Master Shang.
See you again.
—Hey! Lee, what's he doing with the goblet?!
Not sure. But he's got no motive to hide, hanging it off his belt for anyone to see. It's like...
He's asking for someone to come at him?
Ignore it. Let's have some tea ourselves, and see how this plays out.
Waiter! Two pots of tea, and two of the shredded meat noodles.
I'm not having anything.
I'll be eating them both.
Your two pots of tea, sir. The noodles will be a bit longer; we're busy today.
Thank you...
Ao?
Why follow us all the way here?
Phhh... ao!
Hey. Lee. I'm going to ask you something, and you can't laugh.
Did that teapot—just move by itself...?
Shredded meat noodles—
Whoa! Mind it, sir, I'm carrying your noodles here!
Aoww?
What the hell is this?!
Careful!
Huh?
Urgh!
—Hey, are you okay there?
The stuff in the kitchen, the cloth and the brushwork... they're all... jumping out!
Yaaagh! What is that?!
Fire! Fire, this monster's on fire!
They're charging at us.
Hey, Lee! Tell me what the hell's going on?!
So the rumors about that goblet were true... There's too many people in here, let's move!
How do we reach young Lee?
He told us in the letter he left. After we meet up, we find him at the summit of Qujiang Peak. If he's not there yet, we wait until sundown.
Good. We'll stick to it. We can't let the goblet fall into anyone else's hands.
Benefactor, how should we respond to those Ink Spirit-esque manifestations from earlier?
Chances are it's linked to the cup... We'll rendezvous with Mr. Lee first.
Oh!
Aoww—
Benefactor, would you say they've—er—proliferated?
Hm? What breed of beast is this? Why have they been everywhere for the last two days?
We've been surrounded, haven't we...
Ao!
Oh, you're feisty ones.
—There's no other way! Benefactor, you and Master Shen take your leave for now, and I'll handle these miscreants!
—You alright with that?
They act more or less as the Ink Spirits do. Reason says I should cope.
Master Shen! This way!
What troubles you? You seem so ill at ease.
Nothing.
Your brows always wrinkle when you worry for people.
Do they?
They do. Who garners such concern from you, my Lord?
I'm worried for that friend of mine.
What has become of him?
He's gotten into a gamble.
One you asked him to take.
...True.
Are you now remorseful?
I still believe this was the best option.
I'm simply not sure...
You are very clever, my Lord.
I wouldn't claim to be.
I just chose a stupid method.
Is every beast in Yan like this?
Ao?
Hm? Light? Oh, don't worry... mm, I'm happy as long as I have you all with me.
Still, though...
Drill! Keep a hold on it, now!
Completely new Yanese beasts... you might just make for some unexpected traps!
Dear me! What's going on? Did I offend some horror in recent times? Why are my days filled with contesting these menageries of creatures?!
—Oho?!
Wrrao!
...On closer inspection, these things are...
Wrr—
(Damned—)
Close call.
Are you hurt, sir?
You must be... from the other day—
Don't ask.
No need to be so cold, Uncle Taihe...
Would you know where these things arose from?
Wherever the goblet goes, Waregeists spring up. Ironically, it's proving the Sui Regulator's conjectures.
Does this mean...
Mind your words, young man.
As you instruct, Uncle Taihe.
Wrrao—wrr!
(These sprites—er, Waregeists... are they scared of these two? No, is it specifically the boy that cowers them...?)
*Sigh*...
Take a look at these Waregeists. How many incidents like this have we had?
Ten, twenty-five, and a full sixty years ago, with several more still.
While we have wrongdoers wreaking havoc with Originium Arts on occasion, things really don't come any better than this.
But Sui in the end is Sui, and a beast in the end is a beast. If your people carry intent to harm the realm, then Heaven knows how great the disaster will be.
Ao—
The Sui Regulator has no desire to see such things come to pass.
Which is why we need to settle this millennium-long mess swiftly and cleanly.
...No more than detritus.
Those things... reverted back into common wares...
Mr. Nothing.
...Hmm.
Please, convey this to Ms. Kroos for me. I want her to reconsider what I've said.
We can be on good terms regardless. Even if she should ultimately have her own considerations, my side still has a matter it wishes to entrust to Rhodes Island irrespective of position.
I can assent to passing it on, but why didn't you convey this to her yourself when the moment was ripe?
Haha... well, at that moment...
I was a little tensed up. I didn't get everything out.
Lord Zuo.
Well, then... It seems we can't delay any longer. Mr. Nothing, we bid you farewell.
Seems tense?
So a young nobleman who scampers about with the Deputy Imperial Censor in tow has a case of the nerves?
In one or two more branches' time, the color of dusk will paint the sky.
These are the Three Mounts and Seventeen Peaks, though which mount this is, or which peak, is a mystery.
The only thing known is that a pavilion sits atop, Shangshu's scenery both past and present visible from within, beauty unparalleled.
Yet regrettably, this pavilion is currently deserted, and so no one is there to see it.
And on that note, people have come.
Well, here's the place.
What? That depressed about meeting her?
She gets on my nerves.
She's not gonna bug you.
That's why she gets on my nerves.
...*Sigh*.
She's our big sister either way. Let's straighten up.
She's not even here.
Nope, she isn't. Why not?
With all the fuss down below... taking cover up here in the ranges does kind of smell like her style.
How come when I hide you call me a recluse, but when she does it you're like this?!
Hey... it's not like she's always trying to hide.
She's just tipsy and worn out, looking for a nice crag splitting the drifting clouds to lie down on, knocking out for a moment. That's not so bad.
Grr. This is discrimination.
Pull the other one.
I just hope she doesn't cause any trouble for us.
She's always half-drunk, half-asleep, half-awake. It's those people who'll come give us trouble.
Yeah, real nice how she can sleep through this. Is she even able to?
Say, what'cha think she dreams of?
I don't know. Don't ask me.
Heaven knows where her mind wanders. She'll come back on her own once she's bored, in any case.
I hear this peak's apparently meant to be wrecked.
Wonder how hard Kroos and the Nothing guy'll have to work finding this summit at all.
*Sigh*...
Sir, what do you have there?
What comes will come, trouble and all.
You bumped into Shang?
Yes. He stole the cup.
That bastard! How does one man be so single-minded—!
Leave it. This whole thing is a mess.
Heh, good swords always keep the rust away.
Been years since I saw you draw it, sir.
Blades are ruthless things. Best put to use slicing melons and chopping greens.
But if I want my business handed smoothly over to my darling Ye, then I have to settle things with Shang Zhong eventually.
Old Shang might be muddled in the head, but he's not the good-for-nothing who'd harass the Miss about paying her father's debts.
Sir, you still won't step over that final line, will you?
What kind of person could?
One storm, two lives. It was my mistake that let his son die. He has a grudge against me I can't argue with.
I know more than anyone how he's felt all these years.
Sir...
Relax. I'll get the cup back, at the very least.
If I don't, I won't have anything to report.
Master Shen—isn't there any way to get up the mountains a little quicker?
In a sense, but for people who've never taken the mountain passes before, it might be...
You're fine, I'm used to this.
Alright, then. This way.
Ao—!
Gah. What's the deal with these things?
Hey! Lee!
I'm fighting for you here! Is this any place to space out?
...I'm fine.
But...
Pull it together. I don't know why these things keep mobbing us, and we've lost the Blacknight lady and the pole-carrier by now.
Why aren't you, I don't know, hurrying it up?
No rush, now, no rush.
When I accept a friend's request, I see it through. It's just...
We need to find a favorable pavilion near very favorable water for it.
Huff...
(At such a young age, her agility is nearly unimaginable...)
Such a steep pass, and you're not even gasping for air yet.
Have you been crossing mountains all your life?
Only got into the habit the last couple of years...
And I'm Rim Billiton-born. Might not be quite the Yan style, but it's a life on the wilder side anyhow.
I see.
The passes around these summits are all hard to traverse. They always said in olden times that Shu's paths tried you. People argue which ones that meant, but it's all of them, in truth.
Master Shen, you think there's a reason Mr. Lee told us to find him at Qujiang Peak?
When he was looking into the goblet's origins for Mr. Liang, the first place he said he'd head for was Qujiang Peak.
And he asked for 'Zuanjiang Peak.' Only a few locals know that old name; the authorities changed it however many years ago. I was baffled how he even knew it.
Maybe Mr. Lee found some kind of lead on it...
He did have a lead. He said, he dreamt of it.
I assumed he was hiding something, using that reason to parry me, so I didn't follow up... If he really did see it in his dreams, then that's all more curious.

Hmm... Qujiang Peak is a high and treacherous one. It gets the least tourists. It's all the more peaceful here, compared to the well-inhabited Nini Peak with its gentle flat paths, or Shuzhou Peak covered with old theater byways.
You've seen... the few small villages, buildings left, are all left over from long, long ago.
Though...
Is there some other hint?
Though... when Qujiang Peak was still called Zuanjiang Peak, atop this region of mountain, there was actually another peak.
It and Zuanjiang together were called the 'Twin Peaks High as the Sun,' the two most majestic prominences of the Three Mounts and Eighteen Peaks.
And this peak had countless cliffs, the steepest parts almost vertical, like a stone sword against the heavens when you looked from afar. It was a magnificent sight.
If you watched from any other peak at sunset, it was as if the sun fell straight on it, as if someone ran it through by their blade.

Sadly, later there was a massive Catastrophe, like fire and storm linking hands, and the terrain around here was scarred well. Most of that swordlike peak collapsed and crumbled. That was thirty years ago, now.
You can pull aside any Shangshuite on the street of at least some age, and they could all tell you clear and cleanly of the marvel of those black clouds weighing on the city.
I regret I wasn't eking my living on this river yet then. I never got to see it with my own eyes.
A Catastrophe... And Shangshuites'd call it a marvel?

It's a strange thing. It came without the slightest warning. By the time the Imperial Astronomers got the warning out telling the villages nearby to evacuate, it was already barely manageable. There should've been no escape from it...

But ultimately, thunder clapped and the storm faded, and apart from a few lightly battered houses, all the residences in the mountains suffered not a single bit of damage, and not one person was afflicted. That was the first wonder.
Afterwards, the Tumu tianshi came to inspect the altered mountain terrain. Normally, with a wondrous peak collapsing like that, there should be visible traces left.
But apart from half of the rocky 'hilt' left next to Qujiang Peak, not a single chunk of boulder or sign of the landslide was found. That was the second wonder.
What caused any of that, nobody knows. There's just some wild rumors, barely worth trusting.
Never thought something like that happened in Shangshu before...
Yan's history and its legends have always blended together.
Like how some rumors say Shangshu's birth was to do with the lands in ancient people's dreams. The factual matter of things is beyond anyone's research powers, now.
The peak that disappeared—what was its name?
The sunseeker. Xunri Peak.
But Yan's development has flown by. I fear to the younger generation, things that happened thirty years ago wouldn't be worth the time, being so far in the past.
Stay still—!
—Who's that?
(Rim Billitonian) Stone the flamin' fowlbeasts!- Light, keep it from buggering off!
Wrrrr—! Aoww—!
(Rim Billitonian) You just—settle down, love—!
—Wwrr!
Is she... trying to catch that thing?
Seems like it...
(Rim Billitonian) Crikey, just like that, keep it bound tight, eyes glued to it!
(Rim Billitonian) You a Rim Billy, then?
(Rim Billitonian) Now, quit muckin' about... we're only...
(Rim Billitonian) Ya wouldn't be Yanese just lookin' at ya.
Aoww—!
(Rim Billitonian) —Oi help us out. Hrgh! Hey take a squiz at this and lemme know what kinda animal this is?
(Rim Billitonian) It's... some Originium Arts creation-type.
(Rim Billitonian) Originium Arts? Like those stunts the Leithanian lord boys pull? You revvin my cylinder? Had me thinkin' it was some Yan critter I could tame...
(Rim Billitonian) Which galah went and made these things up...?! Light! Just let it go, it's as useful as an ashtray on a bike!
(Rim Billitonian) We're looking for their owner, too. Whadyareckon?
(Rim Billitonian) I...
(Rim Billitonian) You a Messenger's guard? Bounty hunter?
(Rim Billitonian) No sense in shootin' a hound if it's gone into the neighbors paddock, wanna work together on this?
(Rim Billitonian) I don't know you from Bruce, mate, so don't think I'm gonna trust ya
(Rim Billitonian) A Billy, though.
(Rim Billitonian) Not every one of them's so nice.
(Rim Billitonian) That's a dead shame, then...
(Rim Billitonian) But getting to meet a Billitonian out here's still got me gobsmacked
(Rim Billitonian) Hooroo. See you round like a boomerang, Cautus.
Your countryman?
Suppose so... can't shake this dodgy feeling...
Ah, whatever. How far left to the summit, Master Shen?
A road this wide, and you two can't move aside?
Tough.
What does a mandarin want with me?
Master Shang. Do you have any idea what that thing on your belt is?
Is it yours?
Yes and no, but we hope you'll hand it to us.
How do you know me?
We've heard some rumors.
Hoh. What did Zheng Qingyue tell you?
If your first reaction is to think of Sir Zheng, then presumably you know where we came from?
The Sui Regulator's Candleholders.
But you're so young... You're one I've never met before.
If you're aware of our rank, why don't you give up the cup?
Me and Zheng Qingyue need to settle things.
But it seems like those things and the item at your waist are unrelated.
My son died for this goblet. How could I be unrelated?
Wrwwrr...
Fine ability, for one so young. These animals almost seem to fear you.
We ask only your cooperation.
What if I say no? Will you rob me in the open?
I imagine you'll find absolutely no purpose in this cup.
But to we of the Sui Regulator, the cup has tremendous use.
What use?
Bait.
For what?
Those who bring ruin to the nation and its people.
Yan has known peace for centuries. Where did criminals like those come from?
That's need-to-know.
However, we ask you to believe us. Entrust the goblet to us, and you'll have done a justice great as the heavens.
Hah! A justice... For someone of my sort, it'd mean too little, and be more than I deserve.
Move aside.
I fear that's not for you to decide, Master Shang.
This goblet is to be buried with my son. Zheng Qingyue must come in person...
Otherwise, keep shut!
I'll wait for him, at the Field of Forgotten Water on Qujiang Peak!
Ingrate!
—A staff fighter?
Oh—good, strong might. You have some force, old bull.
Lord Zuo, this man hides his talents. No good lies in him.
Get the goods before anything! Don't let him keep you!
Did you ever hear from Zheng Qingyue the words I hate most?
Don't fool with us!
—They're 'save the goods, then the men.'
You haven't seen the innkeep anywhere?
No.
Weird. He's not at any other of the places, too. Did he finally figure to retire and kick back at home?
If only.
What's up, Liu, bud? Something got you down?
I-I'm totally fine.
Why do you ask?
—Your ability's—soil and stone Arts?
Precisely.
For all your martial talent, you're a shameful fool to not apply it for good.
Tying my hands with your sand, cracking my pole—what freak fighting style is that?
Your pole only lightly splitting against me makes it the strange one here. From whence did it come?
I only use ordinary young bamboo poles.
Then my style of engagement must completely lack rigor.
What's your name?
Taihe.
Fine name. Incredible.
It's lucky I could do battle with someone like you at the very end.
You're a mandarin, aren't you? Tell me, what are you planning, here, with the goblet?
Peace and prosperity in the realm for the ages to come.
Hahah, that's a grand statement!
But sadly, I still can't give this to you.
You've got nowhere to run.
So you say.
—You dare flee?!
The pole-carrier leaps one step backwards.
In ten years scaling the mountains, this is one step among hundreds of thousands.
Free as a kite that breaks from its string, he rises as the wind does.
Hmm. A hidden technique?
You won't catch me in the mountains.
Taihe, tell that boy. The Field of Forgotten Water, at the crest of Qujiang Peak.
Don't forget.
Uncle Taihe...!
You couldn't catch him?
It would be simple if I chased him with full vigor. But...
He's using the goblet as a threat, forcing Innkeeper Zheng to meet with him.
In the Field of Forgotten Water at the crest, the two will meet, and one must die.
