Is there no leeway?
I learned its root in our fight just now. There is none whatsoever.
The Field of Forgotten Water, was it?
We need to seize the opportunity.
We're lucky we didn't clash with that pole-carrier any. Seeing his moves reminds me of the last I ever saw Huai Tianpei ten whole years ago.
I assume you recognize those two there?
I told you how my dad's on secret orders from the royal court. Those two are their officials.
Do you know who they are, exactly?
The giant lunk in the front's a bigshot from the Department of Discipline and Supervision, that's all I know.
I see the picture, then.
I'm not keen on troubling Rhodes Island any... but it looks like we'll need to borrow their power, after all...
*Sigh* This does put a knot in things, though.
What?
I didn't hear what they were saying, but judging by the direction they hurried away, they're off to the summit.
And I'm appointed to meet Rhodes Island at that very summit. Who ever knew the venue'd turn out quite like this.
So do we ambush them at the summit first?
Let's not. They're all fine fighters. We'd be asking for trouble.
Well, what do we do, then—
—Wait! Quick, hide in here! Don't poke your head out!
Oh.
The innkeeper?
What's dad doing here...? And...
You look ghastly pale.
Is... Is he carrying his blade there?
No, it's been so many years... he's never taken it out... he's...
He's a swordfighter, is he?
He used to be.
Then it seems he's revived the trend.
That's not encouraging.
Go.
The closer to the peak, the narrower the road.
Nearly there now.
Is this the final path?
The very last one.
Beyond this village, a small ways west, you'll see a long, long stone stairway.
Past its top is the Field of Forgotten Water.
Hm?
Hm.
Well, there's a fine coincidence, meeting you all here.
Very much a happenstance.
What are you two after?
The goblet.
At the summit?
There's no point in hiding it now. Yes.
And someone unaccounted for has it.
Just what in the world is that cup?
If I told you, would you assist us in taking it back, Ms. Kroos?
There's a lot more to your goals than just 'taking it back.' You obviously want to use it for something.
And my guess is your goal happens to be the same as Mr. Lee's. You're all wanting to find its master.
So why go to all the trouble?
Are you asking us, Ms. Kroos, or... interrogating us?
I'm no interrogator, here.
This goblet was made by the hands of a great offender.
Offender?
The offense wide as the heavens.
Court classified. Don't look into it.
But you want to use this heaven-wide sinner's work to do something that involves Rhodes Island operators.
How am I meant to help out if you don't tell me?
My benefactor is quite right.

Though we may be of different standings, there's never any sense in withholding the details when you entrust affairs to people. The proverb puts it well; another's heart is hard to know. Treat each other with candor to get the matter resolved.
Here I was worried you'd gotten lost.
Ahahah. An informed concern, benefactor.
You treat them as your 'operators.'
We do.
The youth falls silent.
The youngest of the Sui Regulator's Candleholders, he must weigh the pros and cons, and make his choices.
It wouldn't be any startling revelation.
But he doesn't want any slip-ups made here.
A fearsome beast dwells in this goblet. It's only a portion of his consciousness, of course.
Lord Zuo.
I'm within my rights. If any ensuing liability falls on the Sui Regulator, you needn't have mercy, Uncle Taihe. Apply the legal proceedings.
Dwells?
Contemporary feranmutologists are in consensus that, aside from some form of corporealized body, walking in land beyond land, this ancient colossus also has means to dwell within certain objects.
The ancient literary saying, 'the beast walks with the generous earth,' actually gives some pointer as to these creatures' characteristics.
Er, does any old object work?
Weapons are fairly common. Whether it's ancient Sarkaz swords or modern, mass-produced staves, we have precedent.
But as for the concrete principle, we're still not sure.
Same goes for that goblet, then?
Wherever it goes, Waregeists spring up. We can't say we're certain yet, but we have grounds to suspect it.
The cup is alive, yet it hasn't come to life. The objects around it are all dead, yet live because of it.
It's been adrift for a long time. It's by a random chance we were able to determine its location.
So now you're out looking for that offender.
No.
I said, just now, its maker was guilty. But its present master is someone else entirely.
And just who is it?
A poet.
...My Lord.
Miss Ning.
The wind is high today. The sun is setting early.
Early, but not so. The time came before I could notice.
How long has it been since last you climbed a mountain?
In antique times, the mountain chains of Shangshu were above human might.
Shu's paths try, as climbing atop the heavens.
It's different nowadays.
Yet for what difference tolls, some matters shall remain constant.
Nowhere in the Three Mounts and Seventeen Peaks may it be found. Will you still search, my Lord?
I need to.
You would balk at nothing, deceive me, deceive us all for this need?
I need to find it.
Why?
Surely you are aware that e'en justified thousandfold, to impinge is still to impinge, and to contravene is still to contravene?
Surely you know that should you err once, not only do you forfeit the future, but you bear the brunt of the guilt?
Surely you understand... I am willing to hear what you say?
I understand that.
It's all the more reason I cannot tell you.
Why?

Because as I serve as a city magistrate, I account for that single city's safety and life. Be it brick or tile or stove or kitchen, so long as the slightest harm comes to it, it is my failure in duty.
As those that tower above me disagree, all I may do... is try my hardest.

When first we met in Shangshu, in my early years, I saw that past your occasional obstinacy, you were principled and upright, of vitality whole. I have been staunch in my trust, that should we work as one, 'twould be no trouble we could not surmount.
Yet in this moment...
...I cannot help but be somewhat disappointed.
These words...
Are they words of warning, from my friend, Miss Ning, to me...
Or a supervisory pressuring, from the Imperial Envoy of Vice-Second Rank, the Assistant Minister of Rites, Ning Ciqiu, upon me?
My head, poor head... what makes this clamor now... hm?
Ah... it was a folk song of bamboo, clapping away.
He shelves his game, knows not of ages' change. From dreams I wake, ask only of the years... what years... what years...
Urgh...
Why is my wine gone?
What cruelty is this.
Shang Zhong feels at the herbal plaster on his neck, as is his habit.
His ten years taking up his pole every day, climbing up and down the mountain, was his habit too.
Qujiang Peak is incontestably tall and perilous. So tall it lacks any human habitation, so perilous not a sound can be heard.
He's lost count of how many springtimes he's now passed in Shangshu. He raises his head to look afar, and at the end of a pass with no human's former trace, he sees but one person.
A person who has been waiting for him, for a very long time.
...In the end, we meet again.
We only just met.
You didn't have your blade then, so we didn't meet.
Well, I've brought it now.
I'll be clear.
I know the fault isn't yours. I also know, that rainy night, it wasn't just my son who died.
Of course you know.
You took in his daughter. How is she now?
I plan for her to continue my work.
Your work as an escort? Or do you want her comfortable as an innkeeper, like you've been these past years?
Either or. Whatever she likes.
Has she found a sweetheart?
Not yet.
A shame.
You won't live to see the day she becomes a bride.
Maybe we don't need to jump to the killing today?
Impossible. I know how you are. Your heart is heavy, but mine holds a grudge.
A grudge of ten years.
So you never planned to win against me today. The restaurants, teahouses, inns, aren't they all ways out to leave to your daughter?
You know the escort is a profession of wrongs. You also know the state the escorts will be in, with you gone.
Don't forget. You've gone outside the rules, with a blade strapped to you. People will sympathize with you. But by reason, you'll still be a traitor.
If you bore the guilt and led the escorts onward after that night, how could I be betraying it?!
My son walked that path. How could I not know? But your actions, opening inns and restaurants? Aren't you trampling upon their deaths?
It was you! You fell at a stroke, and ran from your past!
I did... I know that. But we've said a little more than enough now.
I am Head of the Xingyu Escort Assocation, Asker to the Frost, Zheng Qingyue.
...Polearm of the Shangs, Shang Zhong.
In early spring, twilight approaches. The trees' shadows sway, the fallen snow like silver.
A gust of wind blows, and a blade falls.
You've rested far too long, Zheng Qingyue.
It might not be my rest. Maybe you've climbed too many mountains, improved your footwork again.
It's hard to know.
But heroes like you are doomed to spend your older years unable to walk. It's such a shame.
So your blade flowed quick, the snow's gleam hit my eyes, you cut open my trouser leg, and you think now I can't walk?
You won't be able to block the next slash. That pole isn't any family treasure, you know. It's nothing but a stick of lousy bamboo.
—Dad!
Ye?! What are you...
So you were Du Yaoye after all. In the blink of an eye, you've grown so tall...
Oh.
You two are quite free, if it's getting dark and you still have the time to be... practicing kung fu here.
It's you. What are you doing here?
I'll tell you straight, Master Shang. My friend tasked me to find that goblet on your belt. I'm looking for a pavilion, and I just can't do without that cup.
I worry, see, since swords don't discriminate. If you... glanced off of it in your scuffle, it'd hardly be worth it when the dust settles.
Dad, what are you even doing—!
Ten years ago, the Xingyu Escort Association took on a great commission. But heavy rains came on the way, and they were attacked by bandits in the wild.
To protect the goods, Zheng Qingyue abandoned his fellow escorts. A dozen brothers were killed. Among them was my son, and your... blood father.
Zheng Qingyue let them die without trying. Not only did he let them die, he didn't protect the goods in the end. He didn't protect the escorts' reputation.
I—
And after all that, he even turned a blind eye to their decline, and fled from his failure. Did you know that?
After that, your mother left Shangshu, and went back to her home.
Du Yaoye... Can you swear you knew any of this?
Ye. This has nothing to do with you. Your dad's going to—
Then you're... Master Shang.
No.
The traitor Shang Zhong.
Who the hell do you think I am? You think I spent all my life sheltered like some clueless idiot?
Yeah, I knew all about my mom and dad. I know better than you what old Zheng's done all these years and how he's done it.
And that's precisely why I need to tell you two today—
—Association this and inn that, they'll all be Du's sooner or later.
Now, if you two oldies can stop making trouble for me.
If you don't step aside, you'll regret it.
If you've got something to say, say it...
A coin-sword?
You're joking. Are you going to cast some kind of magic in front of me or something?
Ah, excuse me, now, I'm not handy in close combat at all... it's just...
If you really want this thing, then take it. Catch.
––Hold it!
Zheng Qingyue! No more distractions!
—Don't touch my dad!
Rngh! Out of the way!
Urgh!
Ye!
Shang Zhong, you've lost it! Using violence on the young!
A clang.
A sweep of the pole holds back the sharp gleam, but without obstruction, Zheng Qingyue's blade now sweeps elsewhere.
And that goblet proves its sturdiness again, for how could earthenware anywhere take a strike from Zheng Qingyue's blade without a stroke of harm?
But with that strike, that goblet, black as the night, is sent flying away.
—The cup!
Just leave us! Go do your thing!
But you're...
This is family business!
Fine.
Just hold on for a bit, I'll be right there.
Ye, don't let him go!
This stupid old man wants your life and you're still obsessed with that lousy thing!
Du Yaoye is right. Ten years ago, you lost this goblet on a mountain ridge. Now, you've lost it again. How does it feel?
Shang Zhong...!
If you want me aside, the answer's simple.
Tch.
This is far enough!
Mr. Zheng!
—The goblet's been knocked down the cliff. That Lungmenite's already after it. Move it, both of you!
Thank you, sir.
Taihe.
Understood.
—Cheh.
Miss Du, what's your business here?
I'm not letting you go.
Why not?
There's been Mr. Liang and that Lee man and both of you impossible-looking mandarins, and—
After all my bystanding, I've finally gotten things straight.

How dearly rude, Ling.
If you have the true capability, go bicker so with our eldest brother. Why the cruel trickery on a common human?
Those of different ways cannot plan for one another.
Hm? So you and I are of the same way? Why did I not know?
For how vivid your toast-drinking and revelry was, you're quite merciless.
Which is right. After all, you've thrown away... that goblet I sent you.
Could I ever? It was simply a moment's distraction, while singing to the moons.
I should be the one disappointed. At that time, you had far in advance... laid one stone.
Hmph... hahah...
And what do you laugh at?
Nian's own overestimation. Dusk's timid impotence. Your uninvolvement. And myself, for considering myself so infallible.
You...
...You truly wish to substitute?
Hm? What is it? What are you looking at me for?
No, it is only me seeing through a dull game.
What did you seek me for?
What, we can't just pay you a nice little visit, Ling?
You fell asleep?
Hm?
I'm asking. Did you fall asleep?
How are you able to—
When drunk, I simply sleep.
What of it?
How did you do it?
You ask how I did it... ah, I understand.
You cannot fall asleep, can you? My poor little sister.
Is it because you fear to have nightmares?
If it can even be called a nightmare.
Mm... In many years, we have not met.
So such things are vexing you. And your paintings? Could your paintings not bear the weight of your dreams?
No... is it that you finally began to feel, that paintings—are but false?
You...
Ah! Hey, don't mess with my paintings!
You bundled all those guys below us into your little world, I'm guessing.
And what for... Crude mounts, parched waters. A heart like unto ashes.
How dull.
Gkh...?
But these adorable minor scrawls, these I still find so amusing.
What need you feed them?
You should know what we're here to do.
...Nian.
By your idea, where will our course ultimately be taken?
Don't stare at me like that. I'm thinking about it here, alright...
But right now, I still don't know.
You don't know, then... mm... you do not know.
Not the finest of plans.
Dusk. That scroll all filled with matter in death. It is stagnant, stuck. Somewhat boring. Be rid of it, perhaps.
You don't get to tell me just like that.
Forget not her at the outset, who gave you all true names in one lone word, and what hopes she had for you.
Ancient history by now.
What did you just...
Nothing. Only a moment of bliss snatched, away and apart from this world.
The Grand Tutor had something for you to hand to me, no?
This and that are independent. Even once you see his instructions, you must still behave and come with the Sui Regulator.
All to find him. Find that... basket of blunders.
Indeed.
Take it.
Oh? A letter?
Your so-called Grand Tutor's instructions... no.
This handwriting... would it be him...?
The Grand Tutor is an ever stern one. He would never draft instructions in such flowing script... Did he tell you no more?
My duty is solely to give the letter.
A letter of the court... the first in a long time.
...Heheh.
Is it to us? When did humanity ever get the guts for that? I thought only the True Lung and his little posse would work that sort of courage up.
Then you look at its contents.
Hm?

lines.st: Affairs come in no more than threes.

lines.x: ...One affair no more than thrice, I see.
Okay, I get it. Yeah, we sure do fit that bill right now.
A fulfilled omen...? That decrepit Grand Tutor couldn't have written this!
This handwriting looks like Jie's, but she already... could someone else have copied her, somehow? But why do it as the Grand Tutor's 'instructions?'
It was that basket of blunders. It seems in recent years, he's 'written' no shortage of things.
I thought he was obstinate, that it would only be right he incur Yan's flames... and now this written orders, is another move of his? And why should the Grand Tutor send the letter in his stead...?
Hey!
Unless... the Grand Tutor chose to play white?
Hey! Stop mumbling to yourself!
The three lapse into silence.
Though they have existed for so long, the sisters are momentarily stupefied.
The wind blows from primordial to present, this time no longer this moment.
...Tha—That's...
I—Impossible... Is even this something he can do...
Don't ask me...
But, well... even if it's just a reflection... I don't like how it's looking at us.
Dusk, you scared?
Don't you always have plenty of ideas? Think of something.
Hey, Ling.
We've gotta band together—what are you doing?
Something suddenly came to me. Last I fell dead drunk, I should have left two vessels of wine here... there, there they are.
Husong? Mm... the flavor is still fine to the nose.
If you're so cool, do you have any solutions?!
Solutions? What solutions?
Solutions to deal with It—
—A solution to address Ourself? Need I tell you?
You're not just going to sit on the sidelines this time, are you?
I hardly could.
We meet again.
On this turn, do I dream of you, or do you dream of me?
By her tail, Ling lifts the jar half full of fine wine, and pours it forth into the cup, meant for the one who seems the culprit of it all.
She lifts it to the heavens.
Shall we drink?
I will refrain.
May you all come one by one, and take your seats,
Ah...! What happened?
Where are we?
This feeling—it's just like being in one of Dusk's paintings...
In that case... who are you?
The night parts as a curtain.
A silhouette sits straight within the center of the room.
Behind him is hung half a scroll.
'The Way to Paradise.'
If you have nothing to do...
What say you chat over a game with me?
What's going on? Is it thundering?
It looks like it's storming in the mountains. What's been happening with the weather recently...
Hm? Is there something above there...?
Huh? Can anyone tell what that is?
We've been stuck in here for two or three days now.
If we still can't find a way out, maybe that means...
Quiet.
Hahah... perhaps someday a good mood will strike Dusk on the outside, and she'll let us all out.
That said, where might that pole-carrier sir be?
His son's buried in the mountains. He doesn't want to run in with us in a place like this.
I've heard tell of the grievances between you two... but, erm... must you settle matters by such means?
What else?
I just feel that...
Don't bother feeling about it.
Bystanders always think they understand it all, but people's empathy always turns out to have limits.
I know in my heart what the right thing to do is, what's 'necessary' and what's 'unnecessary.' It's common sense. Anyone would know.
But sometimes, our feelings won't listen to sense.
It's raining?
*Sigh* Well, it's not as if just waiting around like this poses much problem.
Gkh...
Why don't you all give raising an Ink Spirit a try? I've discovered that if you just grant these things a level mind and calm approach, they're actually quite affectionate...
Gkh...?
This goes to show the Sui Regulator had reason to beware her.
Alright, alright, there's not much to gain from quarrelling over them in here... how about we head inside, away from the rain?
Strange how it can rain in here, too.
Truly strange how what falls isn't even ink.
Mr. Taihe? Won't you come in?
What of Master Shen?
What's the Master up to? It's been a moment now, but he's still fixated on his palms...
Are you watching the rain?
...Yes.
According to Nothing, this whole world is just her scroll. Artwork all around, vivid and lifelike.
At sunrise, fowls fly from the steep forests, mist covers the land. You see sunlight without the sun itself, reflections of thousands of floors, and all is silent.
If this is a painting, then who knows just what this artist has seen within Yan's scenery...
How refined, Rainmaster.
I only know that Rainmaster of Duckweeds Shen Lou once served ten years in the army. Who knew he was so versed in literature?
...*Sigh*. I've just been around for a while.
The only shame is that rain in spring shouldn't be so warm.
Go. Part this open.
Wh-Where are we?
Very wise, Rainmaster.
I did it without thinking.
Shang Zhong.
Was this the reason you all fought over the goblet?
The clouds roll above the mountains. What's happening over there?
(Wordless roar)
Ugh, I'm getting a little twitchy now.
Oh... more resilient than the Sui-Xiang I met alone, it seems.
Mere lofty posture. It possesses Its form, but ultimately, has not Its spirit.
We're still here. What 'spirit' is it going to have?
It probably used a similar method to that goblet, but for completely opposite effect, so it got a little of the shape. That's all.
Form but no spirit. In the end, it's just a—
(Wordless roar)
Hey, look at that. You're pretty scared, aren't you.
Wh-What did you say?!
You know, I feel like you've been digging at me with that 'form but no spirit' stuff.
It's been contained with this pavilion for the time being, but if it's disturbed any further... things will be tough.
What a pain. This sure isn't the kind of thing you throw a firecracker at.
Phew... haah.
Hm? Aren't you going to keep at it?
You really don't like lifting a finger!
(Wordless roar)
Ah... so this is what scared you.
I—
O little Dusk.
Don't call me that!
I see you even shiver so. Are you fine?
My li'l sister here hid in a scroll for a century without closing her eyes, all because she was so scared of this shadow of us here. Makes sense she's got stage fright now.
Don't you make such light of it...
Even if it's just a reflection...
To be precise, it's us three's reflections, and a quarter of it's Sui's.
But It's us.
What are we going to...
(Ridiculing laughter)
Grrrr. Rummaging through my paintings. Are you mocking me?!
Uh, I'm gonna suggest to you right now to rein your imagination in. This thing already forged up my seventh weapon concept without me even thinking it...
Just take a deep breath, okay? Save us from getting slammed inside one of your paintings next—
—Paintings?
You have noticed?
The way is natural, and the foundations simple.
Sui was never of such fanciful tricks. In the end, this great Correspondence before Us, Sui-Xiang, is no more than the most unrealistic colors in our deepest selves.
Yea... Sui is but the Years of months drawn out long, and change has been vast. It is on the brink of madness.
It's already mad.
Right now, at this moment, we're just one of Its dreams.
When It wakes, we'll all be done for. We won't just be done for, Yan will suffer for it too.
Ling!
O little Nian, is that why you wish to resist It?
Yes.
Then you surely have quite the sophisticated plan.
That part I can't guarantee. I can only say this beats sitting around waiting for it.
I see.
Why?
Because I don't accept this.
I really don't.
And you?
She just dragged me here, that's all... and maybe I have the slightest bit of expectation.
Really?
Shut it.
I asked after you just now, as to our collective exit, and your reply to me was that you 'don't know.'
This answer truly is not a fine one.
Look now. You are still you, with your own ideas, your own emotional ranges, your own adorations, your own fancied persons, your own fond sceneries...
To live in this land is quite fine, is it not?
Whatever It may do.
Don't we have any responsibility for It?
—I am me. Why should I have much ado about It?
Myriad years the human realm was drunk, and it won't permit me one farcical dream?
So your answer is?
Haha...
—'I mind not.'
Lift your head and drink.
Tail as a brush, ink as a shadow.
Let wind rise, brilliant in sharp anonymity.
Let rain fall, tassels soaked within.
And thus let the might of wine cleanse my heart.
A board game? What game? I'm not too educated in the whole Art of the Piece.
Weiqi, the Go of Yan.
Now that's too complicated.
What say you, then?
How about that five-in-a-row one?
A child's game.
Quite fine enough for me. You like playing this chess stuff, then?
I don't. The chesses are extremely boring.
Here I thought you were a board game maniac. You know, those types who only stop to eat and then keep on playing.
Both sides of a board follow the same rules, black and white doing death unto each other along crossing lines. What's the meaning in it?
Pieces, in the end, are just a method of playing games.
So why fetch me?
A fight of I against I is far too boring.
Wh-Where... Were we not just at the mountain's foot...
Only they would be capable of this sort of thing.
Miss Ning, follow me closely, please.
There seems to be... a bright light in the room ahead.
Really, now, starting the game without even explaining the rules.
What about you all?
You'd really put me on the spot, asking me to play this stuff...
I only know general strategy.
No rush, no rush.
I've waited... a great many years for this match.
Now, what exactly is beating a layman like me going to do for you?
You should wait for your helper.
Lee? Why are you...
Liang Xun?! Er, and Miss Ning...
Greetings, Assistant Minister Ning.
There is much I wish to ask of the Candleholders, but now seems not to be not the time.
(Assistant Minister?!)
Sit, sit.
With this, it's more or less set.
What do you intend on doing?
Having a game of weiqi with you all.
You don't like weiqi, but you'd yank all these people in just to play with you?
I suppose.
That goblet of yours has done me some serious damage.
If that lady hadn't finally exposed the truth... how on Terra would I have ended up?
Uncertain. Perhaps at some waking, you would find yourself to be the illusory dream instead. The bubble pops, and the one who opens his eyes, is me.
What?
Now that's just plain terrifying.
Terrifying, indeed.
You don't seem to feel any way about it.
Really, I said it was terrifying.
Heh... I'll leave it at that, then.
Both of you may think it was random chance, that Liang Xun should choose to involve you in this.
But unfortunately, though it's true there is countless coincidence in this world, for this, you are the sole exception.
There is a man by the name of Huai.
Your expression changes. It seems you only let out that mote of superfluous seriousness when your friends are mentioned.
Come. It's your turn.
I must warn you.
One mindless move, and the whole game is lost.
Hmm.
Lee.
Be calm.
How did you escape, sir?
I thought for a long, long, long time, and realized one way. That's all.
A ko fight. How will you respond to this calamity?
This game...
In the game of weiqi, few can surpass you, sir, is that not so?
I've simply lived for a while. Any longer and it'd be boring.
Miss Ning, what say you to this one?
Don't overthink it.
Clack.
A white stone hits the board.
The outcome is clearly in sight.
I've been too recklessly about, these past few days.
It's not your fault.
What does this match signify?
It's only a game. Don't overthink it.
It's very hard not to.
Think about who wins and loses first. Your turn.
The state of things is evident to all at this point. You don't need to carry on mystifying them.
So you want to concede. What about the others?
I imagine—the meaning of this game isn't on the board at all.
Clack.
Hmm...
It's only right that our win or loss shouldn't be determined in this corner.
The best tactic, with people who love to confuse things, is to play just as mad along with them.
I'm not good at any kind of chess, but I am good... at reading people.
I'll answer the question you just posed, young Candleholder.
There was an accident previously. I lost a... younger sister. You may be young, but as the responsibility lies with you, I'm sure you can understand what I'm saying.
This isn't a topic you can discuss here.
That is true. Please speak, sir, with caution.
After that, my tiny little residence's piece basket was less one black stone.
It was merely... broken, in that accident.
And after that, I thought and thought. About what I should do, and what I even could do.
The current Grand Tutor... The first time I saw him, he was but a deprived student, led by that teacher of prestige past the stagnant city, gazing towards that towering estate wall...
You have no freedom of movement. You shouldn't have seen the Grand Tutor before.
He's an intriguing one. He said something that lodged deep within my heart, at a time when I'd gotten sick of these stone-cold counters.
You seem very boresome.
To the point of stiffness.
Behind Lee, at this moment, are many people.
Friends, companions, high officials just met, and adversaries for the time being.
But Lee still can't see the person before him clearly. This person who appears as a false image of himself.
His features are indistinct.
So indistinct it puts shivers down the spine.
One more move, and even if I somehow understood less about board games, I'd still be able to tell it was your win long ago.
If you and I agreed to identical rules, what victory or loss could lie on the board?
Well, that's some sophistry. You need to apologize to every grandmaster in all the realm.
So be it. All in all, I've enjoyed my days carried at your side very much.
This match is your win.
It seems we haven't quite finished playing.
I simply underestimated my... little sisters.
Our match has no real meaning. Call this my surrender.
I thought you'd be the type who never likes losing.
Hm? Ah... yes, I did indeed lose.
And you, Cautus, name of Kroos.
Nian has an ulterior motive in choosing Rhodes Island.
I wish you'd used a bit more positivity to describe Nian and our's relationship, ya know.
Mm... no matter. Understand it how you want to.
You've no way to uninvolve yourselves anymore.
*Sigh* Should little Dusk catch sight of your squalid garden, I predict she'll be of quite some temper.
You've arrived.
Your tone, to my ears, does not welcome me.
Having so many about you to keep you company with game and talk? What could it be? Have you been too lonely?
You saw through that mirage. If so, Nian and Dusk must not have been able to beat it.
So you had guessed... Kroos, now allow me past. Let me be closer to my brother.
Uh... alright.
(Come to mention it... when'd she ever learn my name?)
You played black. Who played white?
I cast myself as the piece, contested with the common folk, and was restless without victory.
Was Jie's disappearance the cause?
I've surrendered now. You all needn't stay longer. Only, Ling, there's a small present I've sent you that you haven't noticed yet. Once you witness it, come find me again.
Your regard for her...
Has nothing to do with this.
I only fault the human realm for being so exceedingly dull.
Let's continue.
What were you doing while we were stuck inside that strange place?
I wanted to choose a handy staff. I didn't find one.
Unfortunate.
Hey! Are you still about to go at it?! You had so long to rest, and you couldn't cool your tempers one bit?
Lummox! Master Shen! Come, now, give them a piece of your minds!
A change in the summits. This means what Mr. Liang briefed us about has come true.
Sui-Xiang has manifested. Higher priorities first.
I'd better go up the mountain too.
Then go ahead.
*Sigh*... Will you really not listen to what I have to say?
Urgh! What happened up there—
—Watch your heads!
Ugh––!
Stone comes rolling down.
Zheng Qingyue brandishes his blade.
If only twenty years ago—no, ten years ago, he had the self-confidence to cleave apart these boulders as great as his head, not a speck of dust on him.
But he is old now.
Dad! Watch out!
—Ye, my girl!
He is old, and his child has grown up.
No thoughts plague Zheng Qingyue. A rock strikes his daughter in the head as she moves to save him, and she falls off the cliffside. Yet without the slightest hesitation, he jumps down after her.
Innkeeper!!
Tch.
Hey, now, hey! What are you doing—
—Why did he jump down along with her—
Shen Lou!
Cheh. I'll look below, and you get up there! Don't act without thinking, no matter what you see at the summit!
I'll go with you!
Ugh... how... Ye! Ye!
Ye!
Thank heaven, she's only unconscious...
If I wasn't deflecting at the critical moment, you two would have long fallen to your deaths.
...Shang Zhong.
Once again, Zheng Qingyue, once again!
Back then, you couldn't protect my son, protect Du Yaoye's father, protect that damned goblet!
You should know the blame you carry. It's still not time for you to bemoan yourself, and you can forget having any right to.
Your arm is hurt, and so is my leg. You and I are even.
Let us continue, before they get in the way again.
One move will be enough.
It's so tall. You really never sense it, walking ordinarily on the roads, how steep this peak truly is.
People should not always walk on the given path.
Your move just now counts as having saved my life.
I saved the child.
Yes, you saved the child too. So how am I going to duel you for life or death now?
Why you!
Come on, then.
Deep in the forest, white snow still rests. The two face each other in silence.
The sound of a zither passes them by, its notes mournful.
Who is it?
Ling does not speak. She simply, quietly, sits cross-legged, seated upon an ice-cold boulder.
The wind blows aslant in the twilight, the sun about to set beneath.
This pair of enemies faces each other anew.
A zither's single pluck sounds, as if from the heavens. Even so, the girl is not holding one.
*cough* *cough*... Did we... make it out from that weird guy's grasp?
Looks like we're still not out of the woods yet.
Getting blackmailed by myself really isn't a happy experience...
Lord Zuo.
Uncle Taihe.
What happened?
I met the offender.
But if we truly take him at his word, then the three face their own self-shackles at this moment.
You have misgivings.
I just don't understand why the Grand Tutor would trust that offender.
If they fail, we'll be confronting their reflection here in Shangshu. Even if... it's only a small fraction.
The Sui Regulator was planning to pressure Tianshi Bai into action with this.
...Yes.
That's what my father hopes.
The sky!
The shadows are taking form... Is this... Sui's reflection?
The Ministry of Rites has Bluethunder Count Bai Dingshan in Shangshu overseeing a team. This is only the Sui-Xiang shadow. It won't make waves.
What is it... looking at?
Zuo Le suddenly senses that Sui-Xiang is examining this land closely.
Now longing, now grieving, now merciful.
And once a breeze blows past, the massive shadow, in the blink of an eye, vanishes like smoke.
What! It disappeared?
From a farcical dream, it awoke, and so disappeared.
How did you...
Ling.
Hm?
You could do things... like this, all along?
We were there thinking of every way we could possibly save our own existential skins, and you could've just clapped your hands and broken that illusion up?
Our lives are but a fantasy, like a dewdrop or lightning, leaving not a trace in the end.
Scarcely known was it if you, I, or It, like the shadow's shadow and its shade, seem to be yet turn out not.
So, may the one who comes to nothing and is shattered not be It?
You say that real lightly.
It is but a lightest thing.
And that is why I am your big sister, after all.
The Sui Regulator demands Nian and Dusk give an explanation.
Additionally, the three of you cannot act in concert. You must leave Shangshu under the Sui Regulator's supervision.
What? Can't we just have a family reunion at Rhodes Island instead?
I still wanted to pull a couple more in.
(Wait... are you serious?)
If Rhodes Island intends to attract even more scrutiny...
If you believe what I say, then maybe you ought to come see for yourself.
*cough* *cough*—
That's, uh... Well, considering this whole...
I will provide assurance.
Lin Qingyan.
Leizi!
Long haven't we met, Kroos.
Is that a new operator by your side?
I-I am the humble Nothing. Well met, Miss Leizi.
Right.
I thought the Central Judicial Office was unwilling to interfere in the Sui Regulator's affairs.
As a disciple of the Tianshi Bureaus, I simply exhorted my Master's good friend Master Taihe to allow all some leeway on this matter.
No wonder Miss Ning always held her fire... You persuaded Tianshi Bai beforehand.
But at what moment can the Tianshis take charge of this matter?
Naturally, they can't.
But at this juncture, the Sui Regulator may not be allowed to throw down decisions either.
What?
The Grand Tutor has arrived in Shangshu.

Sui Regulator Candleholder Zuo Le, Assistant Minister of Rites Ning Ciqiu, Department of Discipline and Supervision Deputy Monitir Censor Taihe, Shangshu Magistrate Liang Xun, and I myself, must all return to the Liang estate before the Hour of Tzu.
We will await the Grand Tutor's orders.
As you command.
Nian, Dusk, Ling.
The three of you to the Liang estate too, please. The Grand Tutor wants to see you for himself.
Quite good.
Some thirty years and more passed since last I met him. How much he could have changed from then to now.
Alright, here we go again...
Tch.
Hmm.
You are awake.
A daydream.
You and I know well this was their strategy.
That game of weiqi...
All in all, it was by a fluke we didn't lose.
It was but the combined power of all present, facing a lone person over a game of weiqi. No great brilliance to it in the end.
Brilliance...
Doing battle with him, what do brilliance and disgrace matter?
—Ye, my girl!
...*cough*... You're still fighting in this sorry state?!
Ye! You're hurt worse than we are! Don't be brave!
Zheng Qingyue!
Why did you... stow your blade at the last moment? If you didn't, would a hurt little girl be able to hold you back?
You want to run? I humiliate you and you have your peace of mind, is that it?!
What about you?
I'm the same.
Ye, you've grown up.
You've grown up...
The zither ceases.
Ling's figure scatters into the air, like music about the beams. This is no beautiful dream. 'A small present,' he called it.
Now she has a question; a question she wants an answer to.
What of Rhodes Island's plans after this?
Uh... er... huh? Did I ever introduce myself?
What of your plans, even?
I've been planning to get this all behind me, go back and ask that aloof Mr. Liang... to pay my bills well.
Mm.
Perhaps he was right. You and we are not solely together brought for this.
Only...
Leizi, how'd you...
I happened to learn from fellow disciples about the Mount Hui-ch'i business, and immediately rushed for Shangshu.
I might've been a little late, but not too late after all.
That's good, then.
Miss Ling.
Hm?
I have a question.
How do beings like you determine... er, who's older and younger?
Ah, establishing the pecking order.
Within a sea of chaos, whoever first finds that answer, first exists upon the human realm.
What answer's that?
One to a question incredibly simple...
...'Who am I.'
Mr. Taihe, Mr. Zuo Le. It's good to meet you.
Miss Ning. Good to meet you.
Assistant Minister Ning, my pleasure.
'Tis unbecoming of my etiquette that I could not offer you hospitality for your time in Shangshu.
We came uninvited and troubled you greatly. We should be first to apologize.
You came on your father's orders, handling the goblet and Mount Hui-ch'i incidents in the Sui Regulator's name. 'Twas swift, fierce action.
Your insight is as a beacon, Miss Ning.
But Mr. Liang's actions, breaking past settled positions, were all the more admirable.
Were it not for the Sui Regulator's radicality, he may not have needed such drastic action.
My conscience is clear. I imagine the Department of Discipline and Supervision will pursue their own amends once they learn of this.
I've drawn up my report to the Emperor. It's open and fair. No bias allowed for.
As you say, Mr. Taihe, 'garner loyalty and forsake righteousness.'
Indeed.
But the Sui Regulator has overstepped its ambit. No matter the Grand Tutor's determining, I will report the reality to the High Minister.
As one should.
No need.
Zuo Le.
Here... here!
By your inference and in those circumstances, how long would you need to suppress the situation, should the three's Sui-Xiang roam the human realm to harm Shangshu?
And should the bestial Sui have awakened, and Yan readied a city to fight it, what would the price be?
The former would require three days' effort. The latter would be mutual loss, with the feranmut dead, and three-tenths of the army gone.
Liang Xun.
Here.
If I sentenced you to death today, to protect the Ministry of Rites and Sui Regulator, what would you do?
Submit to the law, as I ought.
Then if by unexpected turns, your actions today led Shangshu City to be damaged and the common people harmed, what would you do?
Should I have sought a way for survival, then better late than never.
The game until now was of equal occupation.
The Lungmenite you chose was an unusual, irrational move, yet he seized some hint from it. A suboptimal piece.
Given so, how much more is within his reckoning? And how much that he hasn't accounted for will return to benefit him?
You perceive this matter finely, Grand Tutor.
Liang Xun. The one to take your place as magistrate will arrive at Shangshu within a month. Prepare the transition, and leave with me.
I... do not know what you mean, Grand Tutor.
Follow me to the capital.
My thanks, Grand Tutor.
But I still...
—I, Ning Ciqiu, congratulate Mr. Liang on his promotion.
Assistant Minister Ning.
Yumen's route back to Yan is set. It docked with Lungmen yesterday, to ready and resupply.
Was... Yumen City avoiding Catastrophe? Or...
It had other purpose.
Leave for Yumen ahead of time. When I and Liang Xun finish business in the capital, we will naturally proceed for it too.
Right...! Understood.
Taihe.
All is proceeding smoothly.
Good.
Uncle Taihe? So you long since...
'Garner loyalty and forsake righteousness.' Blame me not, young man.
We will withhold investigation into the Sui Regulator's lapse here for now. At present, only determining whither the one hundred and eighty-one black stones are is key.
Understood.
Grand Tutor, when will you leave Shangshu?
Tomorrow eve.
So very pressing. Need we not await the Messenger ranks' escort...
Unnecessary.
Tianshi Bai will want to send you off.
Unnecessary still.
Shangshu's mountain ways are rugged. I fear it may be dangerous if you are alone...
What, as if wily criminals dare run amok within Shangshu's borders?
No. I simply mind your advanced years. Be careful on lone outings, please...
Within Great Yan's domain under heaven, I only fear if the common people go without peace and national trade is not sufficiently prosperous.
What else should I fear?
Is anything else worth my fear at all?
That's over with.
Past the fire of danger.
You certainly didn't feel the heat.
But the danger weighed on us all.
So it was all your secret orders from the Sui Regulator, having me bring the cup from Lungmen back to Shangshu.
But your status stood in the way, and so did a certain someone by your side, so you had to put a thief's cap on me and get the escorts to act their show.
—But you never coordinated that act with me, which I think shows you never wanted to put it on in the first place.
You know I never liked acting.
So was this your own idea?
Yes.
But the Candleholders were in Shangshu, and they wouldn't give up at the drop of a hat.
You never even wanted this to go down in Shangshu, let alone the Sui Regulator usurping you for a while, them and the Ministry of Rites tearing into each other.
True.
But if you had your way there, you'd be cast on injustice's side.
And so there was someone who probably loved nosing, and probably felt very sorry for you, and he sent a bounty hunter to come steal the cup.
Since then it's a weight off your hands.
You know who that someone is.
Maybe I do.
But nobody foresaw Shang Zhong would come soaring through the sky, out for a life.
Things turned for the worse, and off everyone tumbled to Qujiang Peak and the Field of Forgotten Water. Not very smart work from you, no, not smart at all...
But for you, for Liang Xun, maybe it was a little too smart at the same time.
Which is why I entrusted you with it.
Someone who could both tell what I was thinking, and would never betray his friends.
Should I thank you for still considering me a friend?
When all's said and done, though, I wasn't out to find who that goblet's owner was.
What were you looking for?
You.
You're still that same old you, filled with ambition as ever, aren't you.
Me?
You've gotten smarter, Liang Xun.
But that's just fine. Carry on, you.
I have a rough idea as to Huai Tianpei's whereabouts.
You've had a rough idea for ages. You just didn't want me distracted.
Are you upset at me for not telling you?
I was more worried I'd need to keep assisting you.
There's been a rumor going about the world of Yanese kung fu lately.
Ah, the martial world's rumors.
When there's kung fu involved, he could hardly be left out of things.
Hmm. Shangshu's a fine place.
The plum blossoms bloomed late this year, and I wondered why; so your private visit to this city it was. A rare, rare guest.
When did you master this place?
If wished you did to look into it deeply, I could be considered half of these mountains' master true.
The point in question isn't in the persons. It's in you, and your collective.
When the day comes, what sides you stand on, what ways you see things, which ways your hearts feel, will all determine what is lost in the battle there and then.
Yan is a hard foe to defeat.
You do have the right to speak on behalf of Yan's army.
You and that learned master held the north for a hundred years apiece, it must be recognized. If not for that, the court would hardly remember you so fondly.
The Sui Regulator's deeds may be sensed to encroach, but no matter what, you are fondly cherished.
The years and months tower tall, joys parted with so reluctantly.
To this day, in my deepest drunken depths, still my ears prick at horns sounding battalions forth.
...Nian.
Uh-huh.
The court wants to make a deal with you.
Well how about that. I thought I'd be getting my execution date.

The Chamber of Heaven's Designs hasn't ceased once in a whole millennium. Countless troops and tianshi lie buried in action in the north, yet evil rises as virtue does. The slaughter of wicked spirits is endless, and we've not been able to close the book on it.
The finest of soldiers, the grandest of marshals, the wisest of tianshi, all rush to man the frigid north, year upon year upon year.
The court wants you to erect twelve pagodas and five cities outside the Chamber of Heaven's Designs. Three thousand mechanisms, millions of terracottas.
You needn't do it immediately. Nor will you be on your own. Yan has begun sorting it out, drafting blueprints, and assigning every great tianshi to the case.
I hope you will lend a hand. It'll allow someone to make a return from that tower, at least.
Who?
One who would never fall, but absolutely must never fall.
You're kidding. I heard some of the rumors from Leizi. Her founder was a super-annoying guy... even if she never met him.
Hmph... a special case of a long life warped by dependence on Originium. Such humankind really doesn't agree with me...
Isn't this just because she gave you all that hassle?
Tch.
Ah... I remember her.
Is she still well?
She is.
She's been stationed at the north for over three hundred and seventy years now. If she can return home, the court would be willing to let her rest at ease, allow her a peaceful retirement...
But the man always says he'd hate to be idle.
Sounds like some major engineering work. What do I get out of it, then?
The court will help you with what you want to do.
The wild bestial of bloodshot eyes can't be caged forever.
Hah. It all sounds like a win-win for you. We Yan people sure love to haggle...
That's all on that. Think about it.
I have a matter I'd like to ask of you in turn.
Please, go on.
As of now, is he...
His actions surpassed all feranmutologists' predictions. Turning his own flesh into one hundred and eighty-one black stones... scattering them in the human realm... hmm.
He wants to set up a game across the entire land.
Why?
In his endgame days, he'll surely appear again. You'd best ask him yourself then.
It's a game we must win.
We cannot lose.
Did you hear that, you two?
Would he lose to this Grand Tutor, playing on nineteen by nineteen?
Of course he wouldn't.
Then how did the Grand Tutor beat him?
The Grand Tutor did not play go with him.
Why?
Black and white in their millions upon nineteen by nineteen—it has its intrigue, true, but the Grand Tutor speaks that absorbed within a board of pieces, he cannot see man.
Man?
Thus the Grand Tutor played a different game with him. With Yan's domain as the board, with the land's every commoner as the pieces.
Who won?
The Grand Tutor, at that time. Thus he was willingly bound in captivity by Yan, and with every loss, he would stay prisoner sixty years.
Heh. If Yan always had a genius to beat him, he'd never get a foot out of the capital his whole life, huh?
Who can profess to know? He's very poor at the chesses, truth be told.

Very slow to learn them, too. Centuries ago, in one cycle of sixty years, he lost hundreds upon thousands of games. The following cycle, he lost some less, and after one more, no more than a hundred games, give or take.
And so forth, until a point when no one was left who could beat him.
Thus on that cycle, he left the capital... and so that incident transpired.
That's when our third sis...
Mm.
We could all sense that this was no pleasant matter.
So the Grand Tutor had to kick his ass before he dragged his own tail back to the capital, ready to be punished?
Perhaps it was a good, long while since last he lost in one fell swoop.
Or it may be... that his sights were not set on Yan alone anymore.
So you're Ning Ciqiu.
And you, the bounty hunter of enough bravery to plunder a Tianshi Bureau procession.
I-I didn't know they were good guys yet...
Where's that old man anyway? The Bai guy?
I will take you to meet him.
I think, in this turn of events, thanks are due for you.
It's alright.
But I still don't get the full picture. Wasn't it just a goblet? If you knew where it was, couldn't you go sneak it off yourself and be done with it?
He would not want to hand it to me, so I could hardly take it.
So you got me to help? Why?
Why, I wonder.
I simply did not want him drawn into so many vexing matters, without the slightest self-awareness.
So this bounty hunter really was yours, Assistant Minister Ning.
Hey!
You need not raise your hackles. They are not enemies.
Only...
It was the Sui Regulator who hastened in its conduct; we won't question her deeply. Er... as long as she entered Yan's borders through legitimate means, of course...
Of course.
Go and meet with Tianshi Bai, Blacknight. He awaits you at the agreed location.
Alright. You take care.
You'd seen through both the Sui Regulator and Mr. Liang's schemes.
Not too long ago.
But you seem to not have seen through Mr. Liang's plans to mediate from within.
He may simply have been apprehensive about betraying you, Miss Ning.
Regardless, the Sui Regulator overstepped their bounds in this. We will later address the matter ourselves with the Ministry of Rites.
It is no matter. 'Tis all to plan further for Yan.
'Tis cold outside; may you all rest soon. I will go see young Blacknight; 'tis her first time in Yan, and I cannot rest easy.
Farewell, Miss Ning.
Be safe.
Lord Zuo.
I can only hope this pair in love... aren't shaken by big things to come.
You're young, Lord Zuo. To say such things invites ideas of feigned maturity.
As a Candleholder, I've none of my own ties of the sort.
If I may boldly mention, when you previously passed through that village and dealt with bandits, you did have—
*cough*, *cough*—I trust that girl was only repaying the favor! How can you muddle this with affairs between the sexes?
It was a life saved. If the intent was impure, that would only further serve doubts of advantage being taken.
You presume wrong, Uncle Taihe.
Hmm.
It is as you say, young man.
Not heading back to Lungmen, Mr. Lee?
No. There's still someone I need to find.
Come with?
I'm stopping by the branch office with the funnyman anyhow, and then I've got to get back to the Rhodes Island landship...
It seems we'll be tagging along. But I'd rather not be a nuisance to Rhodes Island again.
No need to stand on honor.
Trust me, it's personal matters.
That codename Nothing operator got drawn into all this mess, had his leg hurt for nothing, and he's still in the hospital now. Don't want to give you all any more grief than that.
That's good, then.
K-Kroos!
Oh, fancy that! Mulberry!
If you're in Shangshu already, I'm guessing Lava got to the branch office a while ago, then?
Y-Yeah... and Lava wanted me to tell you too, it's best you head there quickly. It seems like something else happened...
Ahaha... this trip to Yan sure never takes a breather.
It happens.
It used to just be around Lungmen and stuff... oh, right.
I've been wanting to ask. You said Mr. Liang and Wei Yenwu are real alike, but alike how?
Did I say that?
You did. After we met him, I was even thinking, wait, they're completely different...
Mmm...
Well, after all... both their sweethearts have them by the ears.
You're back.
A hundred and eighty-one black stones. You'll beat Sui to losing your sanity.
While you've already spent all these years in here, in your drunken stupor? No... the years and months mean nothing to you.
That farce of the Xingyu Escort Association was a move planned out by you.
Not totally.
They should be clean of responsibility for me... Why swallow them all into this?
Just as a reminder.
A reminder to me?
Here you dream of all under heaven. Though you know how the universe dwarfs all, the grass in spring still tugs at your heart.
From Jiangnan, up to Saibei, then back to this summit atop the clouds, you've changed a lot. It disturbs me, little sister, so I warn you.
Warn you that the feelings of man are not so pure as those moralists would have you believe.
Sat on equally pitted sides, the brother is in strife, love and disgust and passion and resentment all taken as pieces to the game.
You don't fear them now. Of course you don't. But sooner or later, you will. Nian will, Dusk will. They've become too much like people, so they will come to fear those things.
That is all... this little warning is.
Are you angered?
The cup should only hold wine; wine should not be so wordy.
In that instant, all things halt.
Shangshu's Three Mounts and Seventeen Peaks now have one more peak, and countless more trees, with countless more branches, with countless more leaves.
Wind blows, but the leaves are still.
Shangshu once had a Catastrophe.
The devastating storm loose with sparks, shattered Originium crystals descending like fiery rain.
—Shangshu—once had a Catastrophe!
Someone—offered wine to the heavens, and the black clouds parted, and the people were safe! And yet now, you're going to fling this goblet to the black clouds!
A black goblet. A black piece.
In the face of the most terrifying disaster of all the land, all trace is instantly annihilated.
Are you feeling wrathful? Compassionate? Envious?
Carve these emotions into you—things are soon to come—
Things, soon to come... Certainly, I cannot stay uninvolved... isn't that right.
Phew... haah.
For now, I shall go purchase wine.
I'll be leaving Shangshu.
I won't stop you.
If two people want to kill each other, the feelings aren't too hard to understand.
But if two people want to be killed by the other...
Then we should save the effort. We'd just be wallowing.
I'm planning to retire now.
Du Yaoye still isn't ready for it. And doesn't the girl seem just a little like she wants her own business?
If so, I'll shutter things.
Don't you agree?
I'm not one of the escorts.
Good.
Zheng Qingyue. Our business is only postponed for a while. We're not shaking hands and making up here.
Association custom says you don't talk grudges when you're at the bar. I'll be in Shangshu anyhow, waiting for you.
One last thing.
I hear...
Dad! Master Shang!
What are you charging in here about...?
I've decided! Me and the guys all talked it through!
We're going to Yumen!
